---
title: "bmore_paper_analysis"
author: "Leona Neftaliem"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    theme: cosmo
    highlight: tango
  pdf_document: 
    latex_engine: xelatex
    keep_tex: true
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(message = FALSE, warning = FALSE, error = FALSE, echo = FALSE)
```

# Project Configuration
```{r project_config}
# ============================================================================
# PROJECT TIMELINE
# ============================================================================
# Project Start: December 2024
# Current Date:  April 2026
# Project End:   December 2026
# 
# Data collection spans Dec 2024 - Dec 2026 (24 months total)
# ============================================================================

# Project date boundaries
PROJECT_START_DATE <- as.Date("2024-12-01")
PROJECT_END_DATE <- as.Date("2026-12-31")

# Buffer for data filtering (allow some flexibility beyond project end)
DATA_FILTER_START <- as.Date("2024-12-01")
DATA_FILTER_END <- as.Date("2026-12-31")  # One month buffer after project end

cat("=== PROJECT TIMELINE ===\n")
cat("Project Start:", format(PROJECT_START_DATE, "%B %Y"), "\n")
cat("Project End:  ", format(PROJECT_END_DATE, "%B %Y"), "\n")
cat("Data Filter Range:", format(DATA_FILTER_START, "%Y-%m-%d"), "to", format(DATA_FILTER_END, "%Y-%m-%d"), "\n")
cat("========================\n")

# Check for saved datasets to bypass slow raw data processing
# Place pre-computed hourly/daily averages in data/ (see data/README.md) to skip
# raw ingestion and calibration; otherwise they will be regenerated from data/sensor_data/
local_hourly_path   <- "data/bmore_hourly_avg_filtered_eastern_time.csv"
local_daily_path    <- "data/bmore_daily_avg_filtered_eastern_time.csv"

load_from_saved <- FALSE
hourly_load_path <- NULL
daily_load_path <- NULL

if (file.exists(local_hourly_path) && file.exists(local_daily_path)) {
  load_from_saved <- TRUE
  hourly_load_path <- local_hourly_path
  daily_load_path <- local_daily_path
}

cat("=== DATASET LOADING CONFIGURATION ===\n")
cat("Load from saved files:", load_from_saved, "\n")
if (load_from_saved) {
  cat("Hourly load path:", hourly_load_path, "\n")
  cat("Daily load path: ", daily_load_path, "\n")
} else {
  cat("Saved datasets not found. Raw data ingestion and calibration will be performed.\n")
}
cat("======================================\n")
```

#Libraries
```{r}
library(tidyverse) # Includes dplyr, ggplot2, lubridate, and more
library(data.table)
library(zoo)
library(ggmap)
library(viridis)
library(ggiraph)
library(htmlwidgets)
library(plotly)
library(tidycensus)
library(sf)
library(tigris)
library(tmap)
library(tmaptools)
library(spdep)
library(spatialreg)
library(sp)
library(lme4)
library(multcompView)
library(GWmodel)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(MuMIn)
library(scales)
library(parallel)
library(doParallel)
library(foreach)
library(patchwork)
library(ggtext)
library(ranger)
library(ppcor)
library(corrplot)
library(car)
library(broom)
library(ggdist)
library(emmeans)

# Setup parallel processing (use multiple cores for spatial models)
# Detect number of cores, use 75% to leave some for system
n_cores <- max(1, floor(detectCores() * 0.75))
cat("Setting up parallel processing with", n_cores, "cores\n")
registerDoParallel(cores = n_cores)
```

#Functions
```{r}
# Vectorized function to calculate AQI from PM2.5
calculate_aqi_pm25 <- function(pm25) {
  pm25 <- pmax(pm25, 0)
  case_when(
    is.na(pm25) ~ NA_real_,
    pm25 <= 9.0 ~ 50 * pm25 / 9.0,
    pm25 <= 35.4 ~ 50 + (100 - 50) * (pm25 - 9.1) / (35.4 - 9.1),
    pm25 <= 55.4 ~ 100 + (150 - 100) * (pm25 - 35.5) / (55.4 - 35.5),
    pm25 <= 125.4 ~ 150 + (200 - 150) * (pm25 - 55.5) / (125.4 - 55.5),
    pm25 <= 225.4 ~ 200 + (300 - 200) * (pm25 - 125.5) / (225.4 - 125.5),
    pm25 <= 500.4 ~ 300 + (500 - 300) * (pm25 - 225.5) / (500.4 - 225.5),
    TRUE ~ NA_real_
  )
}

# Vectorized function to calculate AQI from PM10
calculate_aqi_pm10 <- function(pm10) {
  pm10 <- pmax(pm10, 0)
  case_when(
    is.na(pm10) | is.nan(pm10) ~ NA_real_,
    pm10 <= 54 ~ 50 * pm10 / 54,
    pm10 <= 154 ~ 50 + (100 - 50) * (pm10 - 55) / (154 - 55),
    pm10 <= 254 ~ 100 + (150 - 100) * (pm10 - 155) / (254 - 155),
    pm10 <= 354 ~ 150 + (200 - 150) * (pm10 - 255) / (354 - 255),
    pm10 <= 424 ~ 200 + (300 - 200) * (pm10 - 355) / (424 - 355),
    TRUE ~ 301.0
  )
}

# Vectorized function to calculate AQI from Ozone
calculate_aqi_o3 <- function(o3) {
  o3 <- pmax(o3, 0)
  case_when(
    is.na(o3) ~ NA_real_,
    o3 <= 0.054 ~ 50 * o3 / 0.054,
    o3 <= 0.070 ~ 50 + (100 - 50) * (o3 - 0.055) / (0.070 - 0.055),
    o3 <= 0.085 ~ 100 + (150 - 100) * (o3 - 0.071) / (0.085 - 0.071),
    o3 <= 0.105 ~ 150 + (200 - 150) * (o3 - 0.086) / (0.105 - 0.086),
    o3 <= 0.200 ~ 200 + (300 - 200) * (o3 - 0.106) / (0.200 - 0.106),
    TRUE ~ 301.0
  )
}

# Helper to define year-aware ordered seasons encompassing standard bounds + 2026 
get_season_year <- function(dates) {
  dates <- as.Date(dates)
  m <- data.table::month(dates)
  y <- data.table::year(dates)
  season_base <- dplyr::case_when(
    m %in% c(12, 1, 2) ~ "Winter",
    m %in% c(3, 4, 5) ~ "Spring", 
    m %in% c(6, 7, 8) ~ "Summer",
    m %in% c(9, 10, 11) ~ "Fall"
  )
  season_start_year <- dplyr::case_when(
    season_base == "Winter" & m == 12 ~ y,
    season_base == "Winter" & m %in% c(1, 2) ~ y - 1,
    TRUE ~ y
  )
  szn <- dplyr::case_when(
    season_base == "Winter" ~ paste0("Winter ", season_start_year, "-", season_start_year + 1),
    TRUE ~ paste0(season_base, " ", y)
  )
  ordered_seasons <- c(
    "Winter 2024-2025", "Spring 2025", "Summer 2025", "Fall 2025",
    "Winter 2025-2026", "Spring 2026", "Summer 2026", "Fall 2026",
    "Winter 2026-2027"
  )
  return(factor(szn, levels = ordered_seasons))
}

# Function to label housing grades consistently across plots
label_housing_grades <- function(data) {
  data %>%
    mutate(housing_grade_label = case_when(
      housing_grade == "C" ~ "C: Declining",
      housing_grade == "D" ~ "D: Hazardous",
      housing_grade == "IC" ~ "IC: Industrial",
      housing_grade == "Not Graded" ~ "Not Graded",
      TRUE ~ as.character(housing_grade)
    )) %>%
    mutate(housing_grade_label = factor(housing_grade_label, 
                                        levels = c("C: Declining", "D: Hazardous", "IC: Industrial", "Not Graded")))
}

# Unified Redlining Analysis (ANOVA + Tukey + Letters)
run_redlining_anova <- function(data, pollutant_col) {
  # Dynamic formula
  formula <- as.formula(paste(pollutant_col, "~ housing_grade + population_density"))
  
  # Check levels
  h_levels <- n_distinct(data$housing_grade[!is.na(data[[pollutant_col]])])
  
  if (h_levels >= 2 && nrow(data) > 5) {
    model <- aov(formula, data = data)
    summary_obj <- summary(model)
    p_val <- summary_obj[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]
    
    letters_df <- data.frame(group=factor(), letters=character(), y_pos=numeric())
    
    if (!is.null(p_val) && !is.na(p_val) && p_val < 0.05) {
      tukey_obj <- TukeyHSD(model, "housing_grade")
      tukey_p_values <- tukey_obj$housing_grade[, "p adj"]
      letters_result <- multcompView::multcompLetters(tukey_p_values)
      
      letters_df <- data.frame(
        group = names(letters_result$Letters),
        letters = as.character(letters_result$Letters),
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
      
      # Calculate y-positions for letters
      y_pos_df <- data %>%
        group_by(housing_grade) %>%
        summarise(q95 = quantile(!!sym(pollutant_col), 0.95, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = 'drop') %>%
        mutate(y_pos = q95 + 20)
      
      letters_df <- left_join(letters_df, y_pos_df, by = c("group" = "housing_grade"))
    } else {
      # Placeholder for mapping (all groups get same letter)
      q95_val <- quantile(data[[pollutant_col]], 0.95, na.rm = TRUE)
      u_groups <- unique(data$housing_grade)
      letters_df <- data.frame(
        group = u_groups,
        letters = rep("a", length(u_groups)),
        y_pos = rep(q95_val + 20, length(u_groups))
      )
    }
    return(list(model = model, summary = summary_obj, letters = letters_df, significant = (p_val < 0.05)))
  } else {
    return(NULL)
  }
}

# Standardized Visualization for Redlining AQI
create_redlining_violin_plot <- function(data_labeled, letters_labeled, pollutant_label, y_label) {
  # Define HOLC redlining map colors
  holc_colors <- c(
    "C: Declining" = "#FFC107",
    "D: Hazardous" = "#F44336",
    "IC: Industrial" = "#89C4E1",
    "Not Graded" = "#757575"
  )
  
  # Note: The mapping needs to be dynamic for the y-variable
  y_var <- names(data_labeled)[grepl("aqi|temp", names(data_labeled)) & grepl("corrected|avg", names(data_labeled))][1]
  
  ggplot(data_labeled, aes(x = housing_grade_label, y = !!sym(y_var), fill = housing_grade_label)) +
    geom_violin(color = "white", lwd = 1, alpha = 0.8, trim = FALSE) +
    stat_summary(fun = median, geom = "point", shape = 21, size = 3.5, 
                 fill = "white", color = "black", stroke = 1.2) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.18, alpha = 0.15, size = 0.8, color = "black") +
    geom_text(data = letters_labeled, 
              aes(x = group_label, y = y_pos, label = letters), 
              size = 7, fontface = "bold", color = "black", inherit.aes = FALSE) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = c("C: Declining" = "Grade C", "D: Hazardous" = "Grade D", "IC: Industrial" = "Grade IC", "Not Graded" = "Not Graded")) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = holc_colors) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      strip.background = element_blank()
    ) +
    labs(x = "Historic housing grade", y = y_label, fill = "Grade")
}

# Centralized Parallel Spatial Regression
run_daily_spatial_regression <- function(daily_data, pollutant_col) {
  # Split the data by day
  day_list <- split(daily_data, daily_data$timestamp_day)
  
  # Determine parallel cores to use (macOS supports forking which has 0 overhead)
  is_windows <- .Platform$OS.type == "windows"
  
  # Helper to run a single day's spatial model
  run_single_day <- function(day_data) {
    current_date <- if(nrow(day_data) > 0) day_data$timestamp_day[1] else as.Date(NA)
    
    # Filter for complete cases
    day_data <- day_data %>%
      filter(!is.na(!!sym(pollutant_col)), !is.na(longitude), !is.na(latitude), 
             !is.na(social_vuln_score), !is.na(population_density), !is.na(land_use_type)) %>%
      filter(is.finite(!!sym(pollutant_col)), is.finite(longitude), is.finite(latitude),
             is.finite(social_vuln_score), is.finite(population_density))
    
    min_sensors <- if(!is.na(current_date) && year(current_date) == 2026) 3 else 5
    
    if (nrow(day_data) < min_sensors) {
      return(tibble(
        date = current_date,
        rho = NA_real_,
        rho_p = NA_real_,
        coef_social_vuln = NA_real_,
        coef_social_vuln_p = NA_real_,
        coef_pop_density = NA_real_,
        coef_pop_density_p = NA_real_,
        coef_land_use_commercial = NA_real_,
        coef_land_use_commercial_p = NA_real_,
        coef_land_use_industrial = NA_real_,
        coef_land_use_industrial_p = NA_real_,
        coef_land_use_mixed = NA_real_,
        coef_land_use_mixed_p = NA_real_,
        coef_land_use_open = NA_real_,
        coef_land_use_open_p = NA_real_,
        n_sites = nrow(day_data),
        model_fit = "Insufficient data"
      ))
    }
    
    tryCatch({
      coords <- day_data[, c("longitude", "latitude")]
      nb <- knn2nb(knearneigh(coords, k = min(3, nrow(day_data)-1)))
      nbw <- nb2listw(nb, style = "W")
      
      model <- suppressWarnings(
        lagsarlm(as.formula(paste(pollutant_col, "~ social_vuln_score + population_density + land_use_type")), 
                 data = day_data, listw = nbw)
      ) 
      model_summary <- summary(model)
      coef_table <- model_summary$Coef
      rn <- rownames(coef_table)
      
      # Dynamic helper to get coef/p safely
      get_stat <- function(prefix, stat_name) {
        row_idx <- which(rn == prefix)
        if(length(row_idx) > 0) coef_table[row_idx, stat_name] else NA_real_
      }
      
      tibble(
        date = current_date,
        rho = model$rho,
        rho_p = model_summary$LR1.p.value,
        coef_social_vuln = get_stat("social_vuln_score", "Estimate"),
        coef_social_vuln_p = get_stat("social_vuln_score", "Pr(>|z|)"),
        coef_pop_density = get_stat("population_density", "Estimate"),
        coef_pop_density_p = get_stat("population_density", "Pr(>|z|)"),
        coef_land_use_commercial = get_stat("land_use_typecommercial", "Estimate"),
        coef_land_use_commercial_p = get_stat("land_use_typecommercial", "Pr(>|z|)"),
        coef_land_use_industrial = get_stat("land_use_typeindustrial", "Estimate"),
        coef_land_use_industrial_p = get_stat("land_use_typeindustrial", "Pr(>|z|)"),
        coef_land_use_mixed = get_stat("land_use_typemixed use", "Estimate"),
        coef_land_use_mixed_p = get_stat("land_use_typemixed use", "Pr(>|z|)"),
        coef_land_use_open = get_stat("land_use_typeopen space", "Estimate"),
        coef_land_use_open_p = get_stat("land_use_typeopen space", "Pr(>|z|)"),
        n_sites = nrow(day_data),
        model_fit = "Success"
      )
    }, error = function(e) {
      tibble(
        date = current_date,
        rho = NA_real_,
        rho_p = NA_real_,
        coef_social_vuln = NA_real_,
        coef_social_vuln_p = NA_real_,
        coef_pop_density = NA_real_,
        coef_pop_density_p = NA_real_,
        coef_land_use_commercial = NA_real_,
        coef_land_use_commercial_p = NA_real_,
        coef_land_use_industrial = NA_real_,
        coef_land_use_industrial_p = NA_real_,
        coef_land_use_mixed = NA_real_,
        coef_land_use_mixed_p = NA_real_,
        coef_land_use_open = NA_real_,
        coef_land_use_open_p = NA_real_,
        n_sites = nrow(day_data),
        model_fit = paste("Error:", e$message)
      )
    })
  }
  
  # Run in parallel using mclapply on macOS/Linux, fall back to sequential lapply on Windows
  results <- if (!is_windows) {
    n_cores <- max(1, floor(parallel::detectCores() * 0.75))
    parallel::mclapply(day_list, run_single_day, mc.cores = n_cores)
  } else {
    lapply(day_list, run_single_day)
  }
  
  bind_rows(results)
}
```

#AQI
```{r}
# Define the corrected color palette
aqi_colors <- c(
  "Good" = "#66c2a5",
  "Moderate" = "#ffd972",
  "Unhealthy for Sensitive Groups" = "#fc8d62",
  "Unhealthy" = "#d90429",
  "Very Unhealthy" = "#a01a7d",
  "Hazardous" = "#640d14"
)

# Define AQI categories and breakpoints
aqi_categories <- data.frame(
  category = factor(names(aqi_colors), levels = names(aqi_colors)),
  ymin = c(0, 50, 100, 150, 200, 300),
  ymax = c(50, 100, 150, 200, 300, Inf)
)

# Function to classify AQI category based on pollutant value (Vectorized)
classify_aqi <- function(value, pollutant) {
  # Define pollutant-specific AQI breakpoints
  breakpoints_list <- list(
    pm25 = c(0, 9.0, 35.4, 55.4, 125.4, 225.4, Inf),
    pm10 = c(0, 54, 154, 254, 354, 424, Inf),
    ozone = c(0, 0.054, 0.070, 0.085, 0.105, 0.200, Inf)
  )
  
  categories <- c("Good", "Moderate", "Unhealthy for Sensitive Groups", 
                 "Unhealthy", "Very Unhealthy", "Hazardous")
  
  poll_bp <- breakpoints_list[[pollutant]]
  if(is.null(poll_bp)) return(rep(NA_character_, length(value)))
  
  # findInterval returns index 1 to length(poll_bp)
  # We want index 1 to 6
  idx <- findInterval(value, poll_bp, rightmost.closed = TRUE, all.inside = FALSE)
  
  # Handle values exactly at the breakpoints or outside
  # findInterval returns 0 for values < poll_bp[1] and length(poll_bp) for values >= poll_bp[last]
  result <- categories[idx]
  result[idx == 0 | idx == length(poll_bp)] <- NA_character_
  
  return(result)
}

# Function to generate significance letters from Tukey HSD results
# This function takes Tukey HSD comparison results and generates compact letter display
# Arguments:
#   tukey_results: The results from TukeyHSD (e.g., tukey_obj$housing_grade)
#   group_variable: Name of the grouping variable as a string
#   data: The original data frame containing the grouping variable as a factor
generate_significance_letters <- function(tukey_results, group_variable, data) {
  # Extract p-values from Tukey results
  tukey_p_values <- tukey_results[, "p adj"]
  
  # Get group levels from the data
  if (is.factor(data[[group_variable]])) {
    groups <- levels(data[[group_variable]])
  } else {
    groups <- unique(data[[group_variable]])
    groups <- groups[!is.na(groups)]
  }
  
  n_groups <- length(groups)
  
  # If only one group or no groups, return simple result
  if (n_groups <= 1) {
    return(data.frame(group = groups, letters = "a", stringsAsFactors = FALSE))
  }
  
  # Create significance matrix (TRUE = significantly different)
  sig_matrix <- matrix(TRUE, nrow = n_groups, ncol = n_groups, 
                       dimnames = list(groups, groups))
  diag(sig_matrix) <- FALSE
  
  # Fill in significance matrix based on Tukey results
  for (i in seq_along(tukey_p_values)) {
    pair_name <- names(tukey_p_values)[i]
    p_value <- tukey_p_values[i]
    
    # Parse pair name
    pair_parts <- strsplit(pair_name, "-")[[1]]
    
    if (length(pair_parts) == 2) {
      group1 <- trimws(pair_parts[1])
      group2 <- trimws(pair_parts[2])
      
      # Find indices in groups vector
      idx1 <- which(groups == group1)
      idx2 <- which(groups == group2)
      
      # If not significant (p >= 0.05), mark as not different
      if (!is.na(p_value) && p_value >= 0.05 && length(idx1) > 0 && length(idx2) > 0) {
        sig_matrix[idx1, idx2] <- FALSE
        sig_matrix[idx2, idx1] <- FALSE
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Assign letters based on significance matrix
  letters_assigned <- rep("", n_groups)
  current_letter <- "a"
  
  for (i in seq_len(n_groups)) {
    if (letters_assigned[i] == "") {
      letters_assigned[i] <- current_letter
      
      # Find all groups not significantly different from group i
      if (i < n_groups) {
        for (j in (i+1):n_groups) {
          if (letters_assigned[j] == "" && !sig_matrix[i, j]) {
            letters_assigned[j] <- current_letter
          }
        }
      }
      
      # Move to next letter
      current_letter <- chartr("abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz", 
                               "bcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyza", 
                               current_letter)
    }
  }
  
  # Create and return letters dataframe
  letters_df <- data.frame(
    group = groups,
    letters = letters_assigned,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  return(letters_df)
}

```
#Site Info
```{r}

site_names = c(
    "ALotMatter", "Ark", "BayBrook", "Bilingual", "FederalHillPrep", "Filbert",
    "ToolBank", "ConventionCenter", "Stillmeadow", "StrengthToLove", "Waterfront",
    "BUGS", "FortMcHenry", "WestCovingtonPark", "Barclay"
  )

#Site Information
site_info <- data.frame(
  site = c(
    "ALotMatter", "Ark", "BayBrook", "Bilingual", "FederalHillPrep", "Filbert",
    "ToolBank", "ConventionCenter", "Stillmeadow", "StrengthToLove",
    "Waterfront", "BUGS", "FortMcHenry", "WestCovingtonPark", "Barclay"
  ), 
  site_name = c(
    "A Lot Matter", "Ark Church", "Bay Brook Elementary/Middle School",
    "Bilingual Christian Church", "Federal Hill Preparatory School",
    "Filbert Street Garden", "Baltimore Community ToolBank",
    "Convention Center", "Stillmeadow Community Fellowship",
    "Strength to Love II Farm", "Waterfront Partnership Garage",
    "Baltimore Urban Gardening with Students (BUGS)",
    "Fort McHenry Research Wetland", "West Covington Park", "Barclay School"
  ), 
  social_vuln_score = c(
    81.58, 88.13, 90.14, 73.29, 4.82, 90.14,
    49.05, 43.66, 49.92, 62.09, 42.3,
    9.55, 5.27, 8.74, 11.8
  ), 
  social_vuln_category = c(
    "High", "High", "High", "High", "Low", "High", "Moderate", "Moderate", "Moderate", "Moderate", "Moderate", "Low", "Low", "Low", "Low"
  ), 
  longitude = c(
    -76.64776, -76.63861, -76.597796, -76.5452427, -76.6111, -76.591884,
    -76.6312967, -76.616697, -76.6994, -76.6471, -76.60467,
    -76.5967, -76.5843, -76.6155135, -76.6114
  ), 
  latitude = c(
    39.29743, 39.26333, 39.22637, 39.3010944, 39.2775, 39.224681,
    39.27796748, 39.28509, 39.2811, 39.30381, 39.28584,
    39.2817, 39.26417, 39.2619618, 39.3242
  ), 
  housing_grade = c(
    "C", "C", "Not Graded", "Not Graded", "D", "Not Graded", "D", "D", "C", "C", "IC", "IC", "IC", "Not Graded", "C"
  ), 
  fav_infl = c(
    "close to commercial area", "none", "proximity to industrial plant", NA, 
    "central location", "proximity to industrial plants", "central location", 
    "central location", "good modest homes", "close to commercial areas", NA, 
    NA, NA, NA, "close to commercial" 
  ), 
  det_infl = c(
   "obsolences of old style houses", "heavy traffic", NA, NA, 
   "heavy obsolences", NA, "heavy obsolences", "heavy obsolences", "none", 
   "obsolences of old style houses", NA, NA, NA, NA, 
   "industrial plants and obsolences of old property"
  ), 
  inhab_jobs = c(
    "mechanics and clerks", "mixture; laboring class", "industrial employees",
    NA, "laborers", "industrial employees", "laborers", "laborers", 
    "white collar class", "mechanics and clerks", NA, NA, NA, NA, 
    "mixture" 
  ), 
  inhab_blackpop = c(
    0, 5, 2, NA, 20, 2, 20, 20, 0, 0, NA, NA, NA, NA, 0 
  ), 
  inhab_income = c(
    "$1,500", "$1,200", "$1,200", NA, "$700 - $1,000", "$1,200", "$700 - $1,000",
    "$700 - $1,000", "$2,500", "$1,500", NA, NA, NA, NA, "$2000" 
  ), 
  sales_demands = c(
    "poor", "fair", "fair", NA, "poor", "fair", "poor", "poor", "good", "poor", 
    NA, NA, NA, NA, "poor" 
  ), 
  rental_demand = c(
    "good", "good", "good", NA, "poor", "good", "poor", "poor", "good", "good",
    NA,  NA,  NA,  NA,  "fair"
  ),
  within_district = c(
    "yes", "yes", "no", "no", "yes", "no", "yes", "yes", "yes", "yes", "yes", "yes",
     "yes", "no", "yes"
  ),
  district_distance = c(
    NA, NA, 445.4, 378.9, NA, 33.8, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, 4.4, NA
  ),
  poll_sour_percentile = c(
    99, 99, 99, 98, 99, 99, 99, 99, 69, 95, 99, 99, 99, 99, 76
  ),
  poll_sour_rank = c(
   "68/1406", "41/1406", "4/1406", "89/1406", "53/1406", "4/1406", "37/1406",
   "55/1406", "367/1406", "122/1406", "58/1406", "14/1406", "12/1406", "6/1406", 
   "285/1406"
  ),
  poll_sour_type = c(
    "hazardous waste generators/incinerators", 
    "hazardous waste generators/incinerators", "risk management plant facilities", 
    "risk management plant facilities", "hazardous waste generators/incinerators", 
    "risk management plant facilities", "hazardous waste generators/incinerators",
    "hazardous waste generators/incinerators", "metal recyclers",
    "hazardous waste generators/incinerators",
    "hazardous waste generators/incinerators",
    "hazardous waste generators/incinerators",
    "hazardous waste generators/incinerators", 
    "hazardous waste generators/incinerators", "risk management plant facilities"
  ),
  polltype_rank = c(
    "43/1406", "1/1406", "1/1406", "12/1406", "9/1406", "1/1406", "2/1406", 
    "14/1406", "174/1406", "79/1406", "30/1406", "36/1406", "3/1406", "4/1406", 
    "72/1406"
  ),
  npl_sites_percentile = c(
    69, 70, 90, 97, 80, 90, 79, 79, 56, 69, 84, 87, 85, 77, 77
  ),
  npl_sites_rank = c(
    "544/1406", "523/1406", "130/1406", "13/1406", "309/1406", "130/1406", 
    "418/1406", "320/1406", "808/1406", "547/1406", "224/1406", "172/1406", 
    "213/1406", "360/1406", "367/1406"
    
  )
)

# Make sure the category is a factor with the desired order
site_info$social_vuln_category <- factor(
  site_info$social_vuln_category,
  levels = c("High", "Moderate", "Low")
)

site_info$housing_grade <- factor(
  site_info$housing_grade,
  levels = c("C", "D", "IC", "Not Graded")
)

site_info$sales_demands <- factor(
  site_info$sales_demands,
  levels = c("good", "fair", "poor", NA)
)

site_info$rental_demand <- factor(
  site_info$rental_demand,
  levels = c("good", "fair", "poor", NA)
)

# Order the data frame by social vulnerability category
site_info <- site_info[order(site_info$social_vuln_category), ]

# Add missing columns that are referenced later in the code
# These would need to be populated with actual data for your sites
site_info$land_use_type <- c(
  "residential", "commercial", "industrial", "commercial", "residential", "industrial",
  "commercial", "residential", "residential", "open space", "residential",
  "residential", "commercial", "mixed use", "residential"
)

site_info$area_sqmi <- c(
  0.5, 0.3, 0.8, 0.4, 0.6, 0.2, 0.7, 0.9, 0.4, 1.2, 0.5, 0.6, 1.1, 0.8, 0.5
)

site_info$population_count <- c(
  1200, 800, 1500, 600, 900, 300, 1100, 1400, 700, 200, 800, 1000, 100, 500, 800
)

# Convert area from square miles to square kilometers
site_info$area_sqkm <- site_info$area_sqmi * 2.58999

# Calculate population density in people per square kilometer
site_info$population_density <- site_info$population_count / site_info$area_sqkm

# Reorder columns in site_info to include all the new columns
site_info <- site_info[, c(
  "site", "site_name", "longitude", "latitude", "social_vuln_score", "social_vuln_category",
  "housing_grade", "fav_infl", "det_infl", "inhab_jobs", "inhab_blackpop", "inhab_income",
  "sales_demands", "rental_demand", "within_district", "district_distance",
  "poll_sour_percentile", "poll_sour_rank", "poll_sour_type", "polltype_rank",
  "npl_sites_percentile", "npl_sites_rank", "land_use_type", 
  "area_sqkm", "area_sqmi", "population_count", "population_density"
)]

# CRITICAL: Ensure site_info is a data.table right away so subsequent joins work
library(data.table)
setDT(site_info)

```

#Clean
```{r data_ingestion_and_cleaning, cache=TRUE, cache.lazy=FALSE, eval=!load_from_saved}

# Place raw per-site sensor logs (see data/README.md) in data/sensor_data/
data_dir <- "data/sensor_data"

# Initialize an empty list to store cleaned data for each site
# Columns to convert to numeric immediately (reduces size from strings to doubles)
numeric_cols <- c("sensor1_pm1_ugm3", "sensor2_pm1_ugm3", "sensor1_pm2.5_ugm3", "sensor2_pm2.5_ugm3",
                  "sensor1_pm10_ugm3", "sensor2_pm10_ugm3", "bme_temp_c", "bme_pressure_hpa", 
                  "bme_humidity_percent", "bme_voc_ppm", "bme_altitude_m", "k30_co2_ppm", "ozone_ppb")

# Initialize global counters for the report
n_row_raw <- 0
n_7999_total <- 0
n_na_timestamp <- 0
n_dropped_duplicates <- 0

# Initialize merged_data as a data.table
merged_data <- data.table()

# Process each site's data one by one to save memory
for (site in site_names) {
  tryCatch({
    cat("Processing site:", site, "...\n")
    
    site_pattern <- case_when(
      site == "StrengthToLove" ~ "Strength.*Love|IOC_FARM2",
      site == "Filbert" ~ "Filbert|FILBERTST",
      site == "Waterfront" ~ "Waterfront|PartnershipGarage",
      site == "FederalHillPrep" ~ "FederalHillPrep|FedHillPrep",
      site == "WestCovingtonPark" ~ "WestCovington",
      TRUE ~ site
    )
    
    file_list <- list.files(path = data_dir, 
                           pattern = site_pattern, 
                           full.names = TRUE,
                           ignore.case = TRUE)
    
    if (length(file_list) == 0) next
    
    site_dfs <- list()
    for (file in file_list) {
      tryCatch({
        data <- data.table::fread(file)
        if (ncol(data) >= 16) {
          if (ncol(data) > 16) data <- data[, 1:16, with=FALSE]
          
          setnames(data, 1:16, c("timestamp", "arduino_id", "program", "bme_temp_c", "bme_pressure_hpa", 
                                "bme_humidity_percent", "bme_voc_ppm", "bme_altitude_m", "k30_co2_ppm", 
                                "ozone_ppb", "sensor1_pm1_ugm3", "sensor1_pm2.5_ugm3", "sensor1_pm10_ugm3", 
                                "sensor2_pm1_ugm3", "sensor2_pm2.5_ugm3", "sensor2_pm10_ugm3"))
          
          # Clean sensor values, count -7999, and convert to numeric column by column in-place to avoid deep copies
          for (col in intersect(numeric_cols, names(data))) {
            vals <- as.numeric(data[[col]])
            n_7999_total <<- n_7999_total + sum(!is.na(vals) & vals == -7999)
            vals[vals == -7999] <- NA
            set(data, j = col, value = vals)
          }
          
          # Parse timestamps with recovery (avoiding costly conversions if already POSIXct)
          if (!inherits(data$timestamp, "POSIXct")) {
            ts_char <- as.character(data$timestamp)
            data[, timestamp := lubridate::parse_date_time(ts_char, orders = c("ymd HMS", "mdy HM", "mdy HMS"), tz = "America/New_York", quiet = TRUE)]
          } else {
            data[, timestamp := lubridate::force_tz(data$timestamp, tzone = "America/New_York")]
          }
          
          data[, site := site]
          
          site_dfs[[length(site_dfs) + 1]] <- data
        }
      }, error = function(e) NULL)
    }
    
    if (length(site_dfs) > 0) {
      dt_site <- data.table::rbindlist(site_dfs, fill = TRUE)
      rm(site_dfs)
      
      # Clean NAs and duplicates per site to keep memory footprint low
      n_na_timestamp <<- n_na_timestamp + sum(is.na(dt_site$timestamp))
      dt_site <- dt_site[!is.na(timestamp)]
      
      n_row_before <- nrow(dt_site)
      dt_site <- unique(dt_site, by = "timestamp")
      n_dropped_duplicates <<- n_dropped_duplicates + (n_row_before - nrow(dt_site))
      
      # Join with site_info now to avoid one massive join later
      # Use merge() rather than dt[dt, on=] to avoid "unused argument (on=)" errors
      dt_site <- merge(dt_site, site_info, by = "site", all.x = TRUE)
      
      # Append to merged_data
      if (nrow(merged_data) == 0) {
        merged_data <- dt_site
      } else {
        merged_data <- rbind(merged_data, dt_site, fill = TRUE)
      }
      rm(dt_site)
      gc()
    }
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("Error processing site", site, ":", e$message, "\n")
  })
}

if (nrow(merged_data) == 0) {
  stop("No data was successfully processed from any site.")
}

n_row_raw <- nrow(merged_data)
gc()


# Count NaNs in processed columns for the quality report
# (Pollutant/sensor columns were already converted to numeric during loading)
n_nan_ozone <- sum(is.nan(merged_data$ozone_ppb), na.rm = TRUE)
n_nan_temp <- sum(is.nan(merged_data$bme_temp_c), na.rm = TRUE)
merged_data[is.nan(ozone_ppb), ozone_ppb := NA]
merged_data[is.nan(bme_temp_c), bme_temp_c := NA]

# Final row counts for the quality report
n_row_final_raw <- nrow(merged_data)
n_row_dropped_total <- n_na_timestamp + n_dropped_duplicates
total_observed_rows <- n_row_final_raw + n_row_dropped_total
pct_retained <- round(100 * n_row_final_raw / total_observed_rows, 1)
pct_dropped <- round(100 * n_row_dropped_total / total_observed_rows, 1)

cat("\n")
cat("=============================================================================\n")
cat("DATA QUALITY REPORT: FAULTY VS USABLE (raw minute-level data)\n")
cat("=============================================================================\n")
cat("Rows after merging and deduplication: ", n_row_final_raw, "\n")
cat("Rows dropped (faulty or invalid):     ", n_row_dropped_total, "(", pct_dropped, "%)\n")
cat("Rows retained:                        ", pct_retained, "%\n\n")
cat("Breakdown of cleaning steps:\n")
cat("  - Sensor error code (-7999) replaced with NA: ", n_7999_total, " cell(s) across all columns\n")
cat("  - Rows dropped (unparseable/NA timestamp):    ", n_na_timestamp, "\n")
cat("  - Rows dropped (duplicate timestamp per site):", n_dropped_duplicates, "\n")
cat("  - NaN converted to NA (ozone_ppb):            ", n_nan_ozone, " cell(s)\n")
cat("  - NaN converted to NA (bme_temp_c):           ", n_nan_temp, " cell(s)\n")
cat("=============================================================================\n\n")

# Round to the nearest hour for grouping
merged_data[, timestamp_hour := floor_date(timestamp, unit = "hour")]

```
#Corrections
```{r data_calibration, cache=TRUE, cache.lazy=FALSE, eval=!load_from_saved}
# ── Random Forest Calibration ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# Correction models are trained in vaisala_arduino_comparison.Rmd using ranger
# and saved to general_correction_coefficients.rds.
#
# STAGE 1 (all sites, all times):  Apply general RF model.
# STAGE 2 (calibrated sites, date >= 2026-12-01):  Apply site-specific lm
#   on top of Stage 1 RF corrections (once site-specific models are available
#   and saved alongside the general models in the .rds file).
#
# Output column names are unchanged from the old version:
#   ozone_ppm_corrected, pm1_corrected[_avg], pm25_corrected[_avg], pm10_corrected[_avg]
#   correction_method  ("rf_general" or "rf_sitespecific")
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Calibration cutoff date (mirrors vaisala_arduino_comparison.Rmd setting)
calibration_cutoff_date <- as.Date("2025-12-01")

# Sites with site-specific calibration (populate when colocation data exists)
calibrated_sensors <- character(0)   # e.g. c("ALotMatter", "BayBrook", ...)

# ── Load unified RF + Linear models from .rds ───────────────────────────
coefficients_file <- "general_correction_coefficients.rds"
calibration_models <- NULL

if (file.exists(coefficients_file)) {
  cat("Loading unified calibration models from", coefficients_file, "...\n")
  tryCatch({
    calibration_models <- readRDS(coefficients_file)
    # Validate structure (version 2.0 with 'general' and 'sites' keys)
    if (!"general" %in% names(calibration_models)) {
      stop("Loaded file structure is outdated. Please re-run vaisala_arduino_comparison.Rmd.")
    }
    cat("Successfully loaded Stage 1 (General RF) and Stage 2 (Site-specific Linear) models.\n")
    cat("  Available general RF models:", paste(names(calibration_models$general), collapse = ", "), "\n")
    cat("  Available site refinements:", paste(names(calibration_models$sites), collapse = ", "), "\n")
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("ERROR loading unified models:", e$message, "\n")
    calibration_models <<- NULL
  })
} else {
  cat("Unified model file not found:", coefficients_file, "\n")
}

# ── Helper: build predictor data frame for ranger ───────────────────────────
# Handles both minute-level and averaged data, and various column name patterns.
build_rf_pred_df_bmore <- function(data, param, col_suffix = "") {
  # 1. Map model-param-name to reading-column-name in data
  param_base <- switch(param,
    "o3_ppm"     = "ozone_ppm",    # New standard
    "ozone_ppb"  = "ozone_ppb",
    "o3_ppb"     = "ozone_ppb",    # Fallback for standardized key
    "pm1_ugm3"   = "pm1_ugm3",
    "pm25_ugm3"  = "pm2.5_ugm3",   # internal col name in data.table
    "pm10_ugm3"  = "pm10_ugm3",
    param
  )

  # Column names for data lookups
  data_sensor_col <- paste0(param_base, col_suffix)
  data_temp_col   <- paste0("bme_temp_c", col_suffix)
  data_humi_col   <- paste0("bme_humidity_percent", col_suffix)

  # Check for sensor data; fallback for ozone if ppm or ppb is missing
  if (!data_sensor_col %in% names(data)) {
    if (param %in% c("o3_ppm", "ozone_ppb", "ozone_ppm")) {
      # Cross-fallback between ppb and ppm
      ppb_col <- paste0("ozone_ppb", col_suffix)
      ppm_col <- paste0("ozone_ppm", col_suffix)
      
      if (param == "o3_ppm" && ppb_col %in% names(data)) {
        sensor_vals <- as.numeric(data[[ppb_col]]) / 1000
      } else if (param == "ozone_ppb" && ppm_col %in% names(data)) {
        sensor_vals <- as.numeric(data[[ppm_col]]) * 1000
      } else {
        return(NULL)
      }
    } else {
      return(NULL)
    }
  } else {
    sensor_vals <- as.numeric(data[[data_sensor_col]])
  }

  # 2. Map model-param-name to predictor-column-names used in training
  # Ranger MUST have exact matches (e.g. o3_ppb_arduino, temp_c_arduino).
  ranger_param_col <- switch(param,
    "o3_ppm"     = "o3_ppm_arduino",
    "ozone_ppb"  = "o3_ppb_arduino",
    "o3_ppb"     = "o3_ppb_arduino",
    "pm1_ugm3"   = "pm1_ugm3_arduino",
    "pm25_ugm3"  = "pm25_ugm3_arduino",
    "pm10_ugm3"  = "pm10_ugm3_arduino",
    paste0(param, "_arduino")
  )
  ranger_temp_col <- "temp_c_arduino"
  ranger_humi_col <- "humidity_perc_rh_arduino"

  # Build prediction dataframe
  if (data_temp_col %in% names(data) && data_humi_col %in% names(data)) {
    pred_df <- data.frame(
      s  = sensor_vals,
      t  = as.numeric(data[[data_temp_col]]),
      rh = as.numeric(data[[data_humi_col]])
    )
    colnames(pred_df) <- c(ranger_param_col, ranger_temp_col, ranger_humi_col)
  } else {
    # If temp/rh missing, predict() will error if model was trained with them.
    # We return the 1-column frame just to meet the training interface if needed.
    pred_df <- data.frame(s = sensor_vals)
    colnames(pred_df) <- ranger_param_col
  }
  return(pred_df)
}

# ── Stage 1: Apply general RF to a data.table ────────────────────────────────
apply_rf_stage1 <- function(dt, col_suffix = "") {
  # Operate in-place if data.table to save memory (avoid copy)
  res <- if (is.data.table(dt)) dt else as.data.table(dt)
  
  # 1. PRE-INITIALIZE ALL CORRECTED COLUMNS
  # Use consistent keys for pollutants (matching calibration_models$general keys)
  target_params <- c("o3_ppm", "pm1_ugm3", "pm25_ugm3", "pm10_ugm3")
  
  for (param in target_params) {
    out_col <- switch(param,
      "o3_ppm"    = "ozone_ppm_corrected",
      "o3_ppb"    = "ozone_ppm_corrected",
      "ozone_ppb" = "ozone_ppm_corrected",
      paste0(gsub("_ugm3", "", param), "_corrected", col_suffix)
    )
    if (!out_col %in% names(res)) {
      res[, (out_col) := NA_real_]
    }
  }

  if (is.null(calibration_models$general) || length(calibration_models$general) == 0) {
    cat("  No RF models available — returning columns initialized with NA.\n")
    res[, correction_method := "none"]
    return(res)
  }
  
  # 2. APPLY MODELS
  for (param in target_params) {
    if (param %in% names(calibration_models$general)) {
      mi <- calibration_models$general[[param]]
      pred_df <- build_rf_pred_df_bmore(res, param, col_suffix)
      
      if (!is.null(pred_df)) {
        out_col <- switch(param,
          "o3_ppm"    = "ozone_ppm_corrected",
          "o3_ppb"    = "ozone_ppm_corrected",
          "ozone_ppb" = "ozone_ppm_corrected",
          paste0(gsub("_ugm3", "", param), "_corrected", col_suffix)
        )
        
        # Initialize results column for this pollutant
        full_preds <- rep(NA_real_, nrow(res))
        mask <- complete.cases(pred_df)
        
        if (any(mask)) {
          m_type <- if(!is.null(mi$model_type)) mi$model_type else "rf"
          m      <- mi$model
          
          if (m_type == "lm") {
            # Use standard predict for Linear Models
            full_preds[mask] <- as.numeric(predict(m, newdata = pred_df[mask, ]))
          } else if (m_type == "bias_only") {
            # Use stored bias value (Mean shift)
            full_preds[mask] <- rep(as.numeric(mi$bias_value), sum(mask))
          } else if (!is.null(m)) {
            # Use ranger predict for Random Forest
            full_preds[mask] <- as.numeric(predict(m, data = pred_df[mask, ])$predictions)
          }
          
          # For ozone: convert ppb back to ppm (assuming reference was in ppb)
          # Legacy conversion removed—new models output ppm directly.
          if (param %in% c("o3_ppb", "ozone_ppb")) {
            full_preds <- full_preds / 1000
          }
          
          res[, (out_col) := full_preds]
          
          n_corrected <- sum(!is.na(full_preds))
          cat(sprintf("  -> %s: Corrected %d / %d rows\n", param, n_corrected, nrow(res)))
        }
      }
    }
  }
  
  res[, correction_method := "general_calibration"]
  return(res)
}

# ── Unified correction function ──────────────────────────────────────────────
apply_arduino_corrections <- function(data,
                                      timestamp_col,
                                      general_correction_equations = NULL,
                                      site_correction_equations = NULL,
                                      calibration_cutoff_date = as.Date("2025-12-01"),
                                      col_suffix = "_avg") {
  cat("Applying 2-stage RF + Linear corrections (col_suffix='", col_suffix, "')...\n", sep = "")
  data[, date_temp := as.Date(get(timestamp_col))]

  # Stage 1: RF general correction on ALL rows
  result <- apply_rf_stage1(data, col_suffix = col_suffix)

  # Stage 2: Site-specific linear refinement
  if (!is.null(calibration_models$sites) && length(calibration_models$sites) > 0) {
    # Only for calibrated sensors on/after the cutoff date
    for (site_name in names(calibration_models$sites)) {
      site_refine <- calibration_models$sites[[site_name]]
      
      # Mask for rows matching this site and date criteria
      mask <- result$date_temp >= calibration_cutoff_date & result$site == site_name
      
      if (any(mask)) {
        cat(sprintf("  Applying Stage 2 refinements for site: %s (%d rows)\n", site_name, sum(mask)))
        
        for (param in names(site_refine)) {
          coefs <- site_refine[[param]]
          
          # Map param back to output column name
          out_col <- switch(param,
            "o3_ppm"    = "ozone_ppm_corrected",
            "ozone_ppb" = "ozone_ppm_corrected",
            paste0(gsub("_ugm3", "", param), "_corrected", col_suffix)
          )
          
          if (out_col %in% names(result)) {
            # Apply linear refinement: Corrected = Intercept + Slope * (Stage 1 Output)
            # Handle NA slope (for bias-only Stage 1 which has zero slope)
            s_slope <- if(is.na(coefs$slope)) 0 else coefs$slope
            s_int   <- if(is.na(coefs$intercept)) 0 else coefs$intercept
            
            result[mask, (out_col) := s_int + s_slope * get(out_col)]
          }
        }
        result[mask, correction_method := "rf_sitespecific"]
      }
    }
  }

  result[, date_temp := NULL]

  # Ensure corrected concentrations do not fall below 0 (since AQI values cannot be negative)
  corrected_cols <- grep("corrected", names(result), value = TRUE)
  for (col in corrected_cols) {
    if (is.numeric(result[[col]])) {
      result[get(col) < 0, (col) := 0]
    }
  }

  cat("Corrections complete.\n")
  return(result)
}

# Legacy wrappers (unchanged names, kept for backward compatibility)
apply_arduino_corrections_daily  <- function(data, ...) apply_arduino_corrections(data, timestamp_col = "timestamp_day", ...)
apply_arduino_corrections_hourly <- function(data, ...) apply_arduino_corrections(data, timestamp_col = "timestamp_hour", ...)

# ── Prepare minute-level data ────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Ozone: already in ppb — keep ppb column, ppm version for AQI calculation
if ("ozone_ppb" %in% names(merged_data)) {
  merged_data[, ozone_ppm := as.numeric(ozone_ppb) / 1000]
}

# (Numeric type conversion already handled during initial site loading to optimize memory)


# Average dual PM sensors at minute level
merged_data[, pm1_ugm3   := rowMeans(.SD, na.rm = TRUE), .SDcols = intersect(c("sensor1_pm1_ugm3", "sensor2_pm1_ugm3"), names(merged_data))]
merged_data[, pm2.5_ugm3 := rowMeans(.SD, na.rm = TRUE), .SDcols = intersect(c("sensor1_pm2.5_ugm3", "sensor2_pm2.5_ugm3"), names(merged_data))]
merged_data[, pm10_ugm3  := rowMeans(.SD, na.rm = TRUE), .SDcols = intersect(c("sensor1_pm10_ugm3", "sensor2_pm10_ugm3"), names(merged_data))]

# Free up memory before the heavy correction step
gc()

cat("=== APPLYING ARDUINO CORRECTIONS TO MINUTE DATA ===\n")
merged_data <- apply_arduino_corrections(merged_data,
                                         timestamp_col = "timestamp",
                                         calibration_cutoff_date = calibration_cutoff_date,
                                         col_suffix = "")

# AQI from original and corrected values
merged_data[, `:=`(
  aqi_pm25_original  = calculate_aqi_pm25(pm2.5_ugm3),
  aqi_pm25_corrected = calculate_aqi_pm25(pm25_corrected),
  aqi_pm10_original  = calculate_aqi_pm10(pm10_ugm3),
  aqi_pm10_corrected = calculate_aqi_pm10(pm10_corrected),
  aqi_ozone_original  = calculate_aqi_o3(ozone_ppm),
  aqi_ozone_corrected = calculate_aqi_o3(ozone_ppm_corrected)
)]

# Reorder columns (add correction_method after aqi columns)
setcolorder(merged_data, c("timestamp", "site", "site_name", "longitude", "latitude",
                          "social_vuln_score", "social_vuln_category", "housing_grade",
                          "fav_infl", "det_infl", "inhab_jobs", "inhab_blackpop", "inhab_income",
                          "sales_demands", "rental_demand", "within_district", "district_distance",
                          "poll_sour_percentile", "poll_sour_rank", "poll_sour_type", "polltype_rank",
                          "npl_sites_percentile", "npl_sites_rank", "land_use_type",
                          "area_sqkm", "area_sqmi", "population_count", "population_density",
                          "arduino_id", "program", "bme_temp_c", "bme_pressure_hpa",
                          "bme_humidity_percent", "bme_voc_ppm", "bme_altitude_m",
                          "k30_co2_ppm", "ozone_ppb", "ozone_ppm", "ozone_ppm_corrected",
                          "sensor1_pm1_ugm3", "sensor2_pm1_ugm3", "pm1_ugm3", "pm1_corrected",
                          "sensor1_pm2.5_ugm3", "sensor2_pm2.5_ugm3", "pm2.5_ugm3", "pm25_corrected",
                          "sensor1_pm10_ugm3", "sensor2_pm10_ugm3", "pm10_ugm3", "pm10_corrected",
                          "aqi_pm25_original", "aqi_pm25_corrected", "aqi_pm10_original",
                          "aqi_pm10_corrected", "aqi_ozone_original", "aqi_ozone_corrected",
                          "correction_method"))
```


#Hourly
```{r, eval=!load_from_saved}
# Compute hourly averages grouped by site and timestamp_hour
hourly_avg <- merged_data[, .(
  sensor1_pm1_ugm3_avg = mean(sensor1_pm1_ugm3, na.rm = TRUE),
  sensor2_pm1_ugm3_avg = mean(sensor2_pm1_ugm3, na.rm = TRUE),
  sensor1_pm2.5_ugm3_avg = mean(sensor1_pm2.5_ugm3, na.rm = TRUE),
  sensor2_pm2.5_ugm3_avg = mean(sensor2_pm2.5_ugm3, na.rm = TRUE),
  sensor1_pm10_ugm3_avg  = mean(sensor1_pm10_ugm3, na.rm = TRUE),
  sensor2_pm10_ugm3_avg  = mean(sensor2_pm10_ugm3, na.rm = TRUE),
  # Efficiently compute mean across multiple columns
  pm1_ugm3_avg = mean(c(sensor1_pm1_ugm3, sensor2_pm1_ugm3), na.rm = TRUE),
  pm2.5_ugm3_avg = mean(c(sensor1_pm2.5_ugm3, sensor2_pm2.5_ugm3), na.rm = TRUE),
  pm10_ugm3_avg = mean(c(sensor1_pm10_ugm3, sensor2_pm10_ugm3), na.rm = TRUE),
  bme_temp_c_avg  = mean(bme_temp_c, na.rm = TRUE),
  bme_humidity_percent_avg  = mean(bme_humidity_percent, na.rm = TRUE),
  ozone_ppb_avg   = mean(ozone_ppb, na.rm = TRUE),
  ozone_ppm_avg   = mean(ozone_ppm, na.rm = TRUE),
  k30_co2_ppm_avg = mean(k30_co2_ppm, na.rm = TRUE)
), by = .(site, timestamp_hour)]

# Add site info and fix potential NaNs in one go
hourly_avg <- merge(hourly_avg, site_info, by = "site", all.x = TRUE)
hourly_avg[is.nan(ozone_ppm_avg), ozone_ppm_avg := NA]

# Reorder columns in hourly_avg to include all site information columns (no corrections at hourly level)
setcolorder(hourly_avg, c("timestamp_hour", "site", "site_name", "longitude", "latitude",
                        "social_vuln_score", "social_vuln_category", "housing_grade",
                        "fav_infl", "det_infl", "inhab_jobs", "inhab_blackpop", "inhab_income",
                        "sales_demands", "rental_demand", "within_district", "district_distance",
                        "poll_sour_percentile", "poll_sour_rank", "poll_sour_type", "polltype_rank",
                        "npl_sites_percentile", "npl_sites_rank", "land_use_type",
                        "area_sqkm", "area_sqmi", "population_count", "population_density",
                        "sensor1_pm1_ugm3_avg", "sensor2_pm1_ugm3_avg", "pm1_ugm3_avg",
                        "sensor1_pm2.5_ugm3_avg", "sensor2_pm2.5_ugm3_avg", "pm2.5_ugm3_avg",
                        "sensor1_pm10_ugm3_avg", "sensor2_pm10_ugm3_avg", "pm10_ugm3_avg",
                        "bme_temp_c_avg", "bme_humidity_percent_avg",
                        "ozone_ppb_avg", "ozone_ppm_avg", "k30_co2_ppm_avg"))

hourly_avg$ozone_ppm_avg[is.nan(hourly_avg$ozone_ppm_avg)]<-NA

# Apply corrections to hourly averaged data
cat("=== APPLYING ARDUINO CORRECTIONS TO HOURLY DATA ===\n")
# Stage 1: RF general calibration applied to all rows.
# Stage 2 (rf_sitespecific): applied for calibrated_sensors on/after calibration_cutoff_date.
hourly_avg <- apply_arduino_corrections_hourly(hourly_avg,
                                               calibration_cutoff_date = calibration_cutoff_date)

# Show sample of corrected data
cat("\nSample of corrected hourly data:\n")
print(head(hourly_avg[, .(timestamp_hour, site, ozone_ppm_avg, ozone_ppm_corrected, 
                         pm2.5_ugm3_avg, pm25_corrected_avg, pm10_ugm3_avg, pm10_corrected_avg)], 5))

# Summary of corrections applied
cat("\nHourly Correction Summary:\n")
cat("- Ozone: Original range", range(hourly_avg$ozone_ppm_avg, na.rm = TRUE), 
    "→ Corrected range", range(hourly_avg$ozone_ppm_corrected, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("- PM2.5: Original range", range(hourly_avg$pm2.5_ugm3_avg, na.rm = TRUE), 
    "→ Corrected range", range(hourly_avg$pm25_corrected_avg, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("- PM10: Original range", range(hourly_avg$pm10_ugm3_avg, na.rm = TRUE), 
    "→ Corrected range", range(hourly_avg$pm10_corrected_avg, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")

# Calculate AQI from both original and corrected values (vectorized - much faster)
hourly_avg[, `:=`(
  aqi_pm25_original = calculate_aqi_pm25(pm2.5_ugm3_avg),
  aqi_pm25_corrected = calculate_aqi_pm25(pm25_corrected_avg),
  
  aqi_pm10_original = calculate_aqi_pm10(pm10_ugm3_avg),
  aqi_pm10_corrected = calculate_aqi_pm10(pm10_corrected_avg),
  
  aqi_ozone_original = calculate_aqi_o3(ozone_ppm_avg),
  aqi_ozone_corrected = calculate_aqi_o3(ozone_ppm_corrected)
)]

# Filter to project date range (excluding July 4-7 holiday period)
hourly_avg <- hourly_avg[timestamp_hour >= DATA_FILTER_START & 
                                  timestamp_hour <= DATA_FILTER_END &
                                  !(month(timestamp_hour) == 7 & day(timestamp_hour) >= 4 & day(timestamp_hour) <= 7)]

# Keep rows that have at least one valid pollutant or temperature measurement
essential_cols_hourly <- c("ozone_ppm_avg", "pm2.5_ugm3_avg", "pm10_ugm3_avg", "bme_temp_c_avg")
hourly_avg <- hourly_avg[rowSums(!is.na(hourly_avg[, ..essential_cols_hourly])) > 0]

cat("Hourly data filtered to project range:", format(DATA_FILTER_START, "%Y-%m-%d"), "to", format(DATA_FILTER_END, "%Y-%m-%d"), "\n")

# Save hourly averages to CSV
if (!dir.exists("data")) {
  dir.create("data", recursive = TRUE)
}
data.table::fwrite(hourly_avg, local_hourly_path)
cat("Saved hourly averages to", local_hourly_path, "\n")
```

#Daily
```{r, eval=!load_from_saved}

merged_data[, timestamp_day := floor_date(timestamp, unit = "day")]

# Compute daily averages of raw readings
daily_avg <- merged_data[, .(
  sensor1_pm1_ugm3_avg = mean(sensor1_pm1_ugm3, na.rm = TRUE),
  sensor2_pm1_ugm3_avg = mean(sensor2_pm1_ugm3, na.rm = TRUE),
  sensor1_pm2.5_ugm3_avg = mean(sensor1_pm2.5_ugm3, na.rm = TRUE),
  sensor2_pm2.5_ugm3_avg = mean(sensor2_pm2.5_ugm3, na.rm = TRUE),
  sensor1_pm10_ugm3_avg  = mean(sensor1_pm10_ugm3, na.rm = TRUE),
  sensor2_pm10_ugm3_avg  = mean(sensor2_pm10_ugm3, na.rm = TRUE),
  pm1_ugm3_avg = mean(c(sensor1_pm1_ugm3, sensor2_pm1_ugm3), na.rm = TRUE),
  pm2.5_ugm3_avg = mean(c(sensor1_pm2.5_ugm3, sensor2_pm2.5_ugm3), na.rm = TRUE),
  pm10_ugm3_avg = mean(c(sensor1_pm10_ugm3, sensor2_pm10_ugm3), na.rm = TRUE),
  bme_temp_c_avg  = mean(bme_temp_c, na.rm = TRUE),
  bme_humidity_percent_avg  = mean(bme_humidity_percent, na.rm = TRUE),
  ozone_ppb_avg   = mean(ozone_ppb, na.rm = TRUE),
  ozone_ppm_avg   = mean(ozone_ppm, na.rm = TRUE),
  k30_co2_ppm_avg = mean(k30_co2_ppm, na.rm = TRUE)
), by = .(site, timestamp_day)]

# Add site info and fix potential NaNs
daily_avg <- merge(daily_avg, site_info, by = "site", all.x = TRUE)
daily_avg[is.nan(ozone_ppm_avg), ozone_ppm_avg := NA]
daily_avg[is.nan(pm10_ugm3_avg), pm10_ugm3_avg := NA]

# Apply corrections to daily averaged data
cat("=== APPLYING ARDUINO CORRECTIONS TO DAILY DATA ===\n")
daily_avg <- apply_arduino_corrections(daily_avg,
                                      timestamp_col = "timestamp_day",
                                      calibration_cutoff_date = calibration_cutoff_date)

# Calculate AQI from both original and corrected values (vectorized)
daily_avg[, `:=`(
  aqi_pm25_original = calculate_aqi_pm25(pm2.5_ugm3_avg),
  aqi_pm25_corrected = calculate_aqi_pm25(pm25_corrected_avg),
  aqi_pm10_original = calculate_aqi_pm10(pm10_ugm3_avg),
  aqi_pm10_corrected = calculate_aqi_pm10(pm10_corrected_avg),
  aqi_ozone_original = calculate_aqi_o3(ozone_ppm_avg),
  aqi_ozone_corrected = calculate_aqi_o3(ozone_ppm_corrected)
)]

# Show sample of corrected data
cat("\nSample of corrected daily data:\n")
print(head(daily_avg[, .(timestamp_day, site, ozone_ppm_avg, ozone_ppm_corrected, 
                         pm2.5_ugm3_avg, pm25_corrected_avg, pm10_ugm3_avg, pm10_corrected_avg)], 5))

# Summary of corrections applied
cat("\nDaily Correction Summary:\n")
cat("- Ozone: Original range", range(daily_avg$ozone_ppm_avg, na.rm = TRUE), 
    "→ Corrected range", range(daily_avg$ozone_ppm_corrected, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("- PM2.5: Original range", range(daily_avg$pm2.5_ugm3_avg, na.rm = TRUE), 
    "→ Corrected range", range(daily_avg$pm25_corrected_avg, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("- PM10: Original range", range(daily_avg$pm10_ugm3_avg, na.rm = TRUE), 
    "→ Corrected range", range(daily_avg$pm10_corrected_avg, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")

# Filter to project date range (excluding July 4-7 holiday period)
daily_avg <- daily_avg[timestamp_day >= DATA_FILTER_START & timestamp_day <= DATA_FILTER_END &
                        !(month(timestamp_day) == 7 & day(timestamp_day) >= 4 & day(timestamp_day) <= 7)]

# Keep rows that have at least one valid pollutant or temperature measurement
essential_cols_daily <- c("ozone_ppm_avg", "pm2.5_ugm3_avg", "pm10_ugm3_avg", "bme_temp_c_avg")
daily_avg <- daily_avg[rowSums(!is.na(daily_avg[, ..essential_cols_daily])) > 0]

cat("Daily data filtered to project range:", format(DATA_FILTER_START, "%Y-%m-%d"), "to", format(DATA_FILTER_END, "%Y-%m-%d"), "\n")

# Save daily averages to CSV
if (!dir.exists("data")) {
  dir.create("data", recursive = TRUE)
}
data.table::fwrite(daily_avg, local_daily_path)
cat("Saved daily averages to", local_daily_path, "\n")

# Centralize labeling and factors for all pollutant analyses
daily_avg <- daily_avg %>%
  label_housing_grades() %>%  # Uses new shared function
  mutate(
    month_num = month(timestamp_day),
    season_base = case_when(
      month_num %in% c(12, 1, 2)  ~ "Winter",
      month_num %in% c(3, 4, 5)   ~ "Spring", 
      month_num %in% c(6, 7, 8)   ~ "Summer",
      month_num %in% c(9, 10, 11) ~ "Fall"
    ),
    season = get_season_year(timestamp_day) # Uses new shared function
  )

# Ensure housing_grade is a factor with proper levels for ANOVA
daily_avg$housing_grade <- factor(daily_avg$housing_grade, 
                                 levels = c("A", "B", "C", "D", "IC", "Not Graded"))

# Create numeric version for Spearman correlations and GWR
daily_avg$housing_grade_numeric <- as.numeric(factor(daily_avg$housing_grade, 
                                                    levels = c("IC", "D", "C", "Not Graded"), 
                                                    ordered = TRUE))
```

```{r load_saved_data, eval=load_from_saved}
cat("=== LOADING SAVED DATASETS ===\n")
library(data.table)
library(dplyr)

# Load hourly averages
hourly_avg <- data.table::fread(hourly_load_path)
if (!inherits(hourly_avg$timestamp_hour, "POSIXct")) {
  hourly_avg[, timestamp_hour := lubridate::ymd_hms(timestamp_hour, tz = "America/New_York")]
} else {
  hourly_avg[, timestamp_hour := lubridate::with_tz(timestamp_hour, tzone = "America/New_York")]
}
cat("Loaded hourly averages from:", hourly_load_path, "\n")

# Load daily averages
daily_avg <- data.table::fread(daily_load_path)
if (inherits(daily_avg$timestamp_day, "POSIXct")) {
  daily_avg[, timestamp_day := as.Date(lubridate::with_tz(timestamp_day, tzone = "America/New_York"))]
} else {
  daily_avg[, timestamp_day := as.Date(timestamp_day)]
}
cat("Loaded daily averages from:", daily_load_path, "\n")

# Re-apply factor formatting and labeling to daily_avg to ensure downstream compatibility
daily_avg <- daily_avg %>%
  label_housing_grades() %>%  # Uses new shared function
  mutate(
    month_num = month(timestamp_day),
    season_base = case_when(
      month_num %in% c(12, 1, 2)  ~ "Winter",
      month_num %in% c(3, 4, 5)   ~ "Spring", 
      month_num %in% c(6, 7, 8)   ~ "Summer",
      month_num %in% c(9, 10, 11) ~ "Fall"
    ),
    season = get_season_year(timestamp_day) # Uses new shared function
  )

# Ensure housing_grade is a factor with proper levels for ANOVA
daily_avg$housing_grade <- factor(daily_avg$housing_grade, 
                                 levels = c("A", "B", "C", "D", "IC", "Not Graded"))

# Create numeric version for Spearman correlations and GWR
daily_avg$housing_grade_numeric <- as.numeric(factor(daily_avg$housing_grade, 
                                                    levels = c("IC", "D", "C", "Not Graded"), 
                                                    ordered = TRUE))
cat("Centralized labeling and factor properties initialized on daily_avg.\n")
```

#Stats
##1. Redlining and temp

```{r}
library(corrplot)
library(car)
library(broom)

# Setup: Define grade order and HOLC colors
grade_order <- c("C: Declining", "D: Hazardous", "IC: Industrial", "Not Graded")
grade_colors <- c(
  "C: Declining" = "#FFC107",     # Yellow/Amber (HOLC C)
  "D: Hazardous" = "#F44336",     # Red (HOLC D)
  "IC: Industrial" = "#89C4E1",   # Light Blue (HOLC IC)
  "Not Graded" = "#757575"        # Dark Gray (Not Graded)
)

# Prepare temperature analysis dataset
temp_data_all <- daily_avg %>%
  filter(!is.na(bme_temp_c_avg), !is.na(housing_grade), 
         !is.na(population_density))

original_n <- nrow(temp_data_all)

# APPLY FILTER: Remove impossible temperatures below 20°C in summer
temp_data <- temp_data_all %>%
  filter(!(season_base == "Summer" & bme_temp_c_avg < 20))

anomalies_removed <- original_n - nrow(temp_data)

# Report data cleaning
cat("DATA QUALITY REPORT:\n")
cat("Original observations (valid temp/grade/use/dens):", original_n, "\n")
cat("Anomalous summer readings removed:", anomalies_removed, "\n")
cat("Final dataset size:", nrow(temp_data), "\n\n")

# STATISTICAL ANALYSIS
cat("STATISTICAL ANALYSIS:\n")
cat("====================\n")

# ANOVA Model (HOLC Grade → Temperature) WITH CONTROLS
cat("ANOVA Model (HOLC Grade → Temperature) WITH CONTROLS:\n")

# Safety check: Verify we have enough data and levels for the model
h_levels <- n_distinct(temp_data$housing_grade_label)
if (nrow(temp_data) > 10 && h_levels >= 2) {
  temp_anova_controlled <- aov(bme_temp_c_avg ~ housing_grade_label + population_density, data = temp_data)
  anova_summary_controlled <- summary(temp_anova_controlled)
  print(anova_summary_controlled)
  
  # Post-hoc Tukey HSD test for HOLC grade (controlling for population density)
  cat("\nPost-hoc Tukey HSD test (controlling for population density):\n")
  tukey_results_controlled <- TukeyHSD(temp_anova_controlled, "housing_grade_label")
  print(tukey_results_controlled)
} else {
  cat("Insufficient data or factor levels to run ANOVA model.\n")
  cat("Rows:", nrow(temp_data), "| Housing Grade levels:", h_levels, "\n")
}

# Generate significance letters for temperature analysis using multcompView
# Extract p-values from Tukey result
tukey_p_values <- tukey_results_controlled$housing_grade_label[, "p adj"]

# Use multcompLetters which correctly handles compact letter display
letters_result <- multcompLetters(tukey_p_values)

# Create dataframe matching group names to letters
# The letters_result contains letters for each comparison, we need to extract unique groups
letters_df_controlled <- data.frame(
  group = names(letters_result$Letters),
  letters = as.character(letters_result$Letters),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Debug: Print temperature letters
cat("Temperature significance letters:\n")
print(letters_df_controlled)

# Calculate positions for significance letters
y_positions_controlled <- temp_data %>%
  group_by(housing_grade_label) %>%
  summarise(y_pos = max(bme_temp_c_avg, na.rm = TRUE) + 5)

annotation_df_controlled <- left_join(letters_df_controlled, y_positions_controlled, by = c("group" = "housing_grade_label"))

# Calculate median and mean temperatures for each grade
temp_summary_stats <- temp_data %>%
  group_by(housing_grade_label) %>%
  summarise(
    median_temp = median(bme_temp_c_avg, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_temp = mean(bme_temp_c_avg, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_temp = min(bme_temp_c_avg, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Position labels slightly below the minimum to avoid overlap with violin
    label_y_pos = min_temp - 3,
    # Format labels to show median with 1 decimal place
    temp_label = paste0("Median: ", round(median_temp, 1), "°C")
  )

# Print summary statistics
cat("\nTemperature Summary Statistics by Housing Grade:\n")
print(temp_summary_stats)

# CREATE PUBLICATION-READY PLOT
temp_plot_controlled <- ggplot(temp_data, aes(x = housing_grade_label, y = bme_temp_c_avg)) +
  geom_violin(aes(fill = housing_grade_label), 
              color = "white", lwd = 1, alpha = 0.8, trim = FALSE) +
  stat_summary(fun = median, geom = "point", shape = 21, size = 3.5, 
               fill = "white", color = "black", stroke = 1.2) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.18, alpha = 0.15, size = 0.8, color = "black") +
  # Add significance letters at top
  geom_text(
    data = annotation_df_controlled %>% mutate(y_pos = y_pos + 5),
    aes(x = group, y = y_pos, label = letters), 
    size = 7, fontface = "bold", color = "black", vjust = 0
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("C: Declining" = "Grade C", "D: Hazardous" = "Grade D", "IC: Industrial" = "Grade IC", "Not Graded" = "Not Graded")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = grade_colors) +
  annotate("text", x = -Inf, y = Inf, label = "(a)", hjust = -0.5, vjust = 1.5, size = 5, fontface = "bold") +
  labs(
    x = "Historic housing grade",
    y = "Daily average temperature (°C)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, vjust = 1, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(min(temp_data$bme_temp_c_avg, na.rm = TRUE) - 10, max(temp_data$bme_temp_c_avg, na.rm = TRUE) + 15), expand = FALSE)

print(temp_plot_controlled)

# SUMMARY AND INTERPRETATION
cat("\n=== SUMMARY WITH CONTROLS ===\n")
cat("✓ ANOVA Results (with controls): F =", round(anova_summary_controlled[[1]][["F value"]][1], 2), ", p =", round(anova_summary_controlled[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1], 4), "\n")

if (anova_summary_controlled[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1] < 0.05) {
  cat("✓ Significant HOLC grade effect on temperature (controlling for population density)\n")
} else {
  cat("✓ No significant HOLC grade effect on temperature (controlling for population density)\n")
}

# Check control variable effects
pop_density_p <- anova_summary_controlled[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][2]

cat("✓ Population density effect: p =", round(pop_density_p, 4), "\n")

# Quick correlation check (controlling for population density only)
# Partial correlation controlling for population density only (land_use_type is categorical)
library(ppcor) # Already loaded at top
# Filter out missing values for partial correlation
temp_data_complete <- temp_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(bme_temp_c_avg), !is.na(housing_grade_numeric), !is.na(population_density))

if (nrow(temp_data_complete) > 0) {
  temp_partial_cor <- pcor.test(temp_data_complete$bme_temp_c_avg, temp_data_complete$housing_grade_numeric, 
                                temp_data_complete[, c("population_density")], method = "spearman")
  cat("✓ Partial Spearman correlation (controlling for population density): ρ =", round(temp_partial_cor$estimate, 3), ", p =", round(temp_partial_cor$p.value, 4), "\n")
} else {
  cat("✓ Insufficient complete data for partial correlation analysis\n")
}

cat("\nKey Finding: Grade C (declining) areas show the highest temperatures, even after controlling for land use type and population density.\n")

# 1C. GWR: Temperature by Historic Redlining (WITH CONTROLS)
cat("\n1C. GWR: Temperature by Historic Redlining (WITH CONTROLS)\n")
cat("========================================================\n")

# Prepare spatial data for GWR
# Prepare spatial data for GWR by aggregating to site-level averages to avoid duplicate coordinates and massive distance matrices
temp_spatial_controlled <- temp_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(longitude), !is.na(latitude)) %>%
  group_by(site, longitude, latitude) %>%
  summarise(
    bme_temp_c_avg = mean(bme_temp_c_avg, na.rm = TRUE),
    housing_grade_numeric = mean(housing_grade_numeric, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  as.data.frame()

coordinates(temp_spatial_controlled) <- ~longitude + latitude
proj4string(temp_spatial_controlled) <- CRS("+proj=longlat +datum=WGS84")

# Run GWR for temperature with controls (simplified approach)
tryCatch({
  # Use a simpler model to avoid matrix dimension issues
  bw_temp_controlled <- bw.gwr(bme_temp_c_avg ~ housing_grade_numeric, 
                              data = temp_spatial_controlled, 
                              approach = "AIC",
                              kernel = "gaussian",
                              adaptive = TRUE)
  
  gwr_temp_controlled <- gwr.basic(bme_temp_c_avg ~ housing_grade_numeric, 
                                  data = temp_spatial_controlled,
                                  bw = bw_temp_controlled,
                                  kernel = "gaussian",
                                  adaptive = TRUE)
  
  cat("GWR Results Summary (with controls - simplified model):\n")
  cat("Bandwidth:", bw_temp_controlled, "\n")
  cat("Global R-squared:", gwr_temp_controlled$GW.diagnostic$gwR2, "\n")
  cat("Local R-squared range:", range(gwr_temp_controlled$SDF$Local_R2), "\n")
  
  # Extract coefficients
  gwr_temp_coefs_controlled <- data.frame(
    site = temp_spatial_controlled$site,
    longitude = coordinates(temp_spatial_controlled)[,1],
    latitude = coordinates(temp_spatial_controlled)[,2],
    housing_coef = gwr_temp_controlled$SDF$housing_grade_numeric,
    local_r2 = gwr_temp_controlled$SDF$Local_R2
  )
  
  cat("Housing grade coefficient summary (simplified model):\n")
  print(summary(gwr_temp_coefs_controlled$housing_coef))
  
  # Visualization: Spatial map of GWR coefficients
  gwr_temp_map_controlled <- ggplot(gwr_temp_coefs_controlled, aes(x = longitude, y = latitude)) +
    geom_point(aes(color = housing_coef, size = abs(housing_coef))) +
    scale_color_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red", midpoint = 0,
                         name = "Housing Grade\nCoefficient") +
    scale_size_continuous(name = "|Coefficient|") +
    labs(
      title = "GWR: Spatial Variation in Housing Grade Effect on Temperature",
      subtitle = paste("Simplified model | Global R² =", round(gwr_temp_controlled$GW.diagnostic$gwR2, 3)),
      x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude"
    ) +
    theme_minimal()
  
  # print(gwr_temp_map_controlled)  # moved to supplementary
  
}, error = function(e) {
  cat("GWR failed for temperature analysis with controls:", e$message, "\n")
  cat("Note: GWR analysis may be limited due to data structure or spatial distribution.\n")
})

```
##3. Redlining + AQI
```{r}
cat("\n=============================================================================\n")
cat("4. HISTORIC REDLINING AND AQI\n")
cat("=============================================================================\n\n")

### 4A. REDLINING AND PM2.5 AQI ###
cat("\n4A. REDLINING AND PM2.5 AQI\n")
cat("=============================\n\n")

# Prepare PM2.5 redlining data
pm25_redlining <- daily_avg %>%
  filter(!is.na(aqi_pm25_corrected)) %>%
  # Clean: Set any negative AQI values to 0 (result of corrections)
  mutate(aqi_pm25_corrected = ifelse(aqi_pm25_corrected < 0, 0, aqi_pm25_corrected))

# 4A.1. Spearman Correlation: Historic Redlining vs PM2.5 AQI (WITH CONTROLS)
cat("4A.1. Spearman Correlation (Partial) - Controls: Pop Density\n")
library(ppcor) # Already loaded at top
pm25_cor_data <- pm25_redlining %>% 
  filter(!is.na(housing_grade_numeric), !is.na(population_density))

if (nrow(pm25_cor_data) > 5) {
  print(pcor.test(pm25_cor_data$aqi_pm25_corrected, pm25_cor_data$housing_grade_numeric, 
                  pm25_cor_data[, c("population_density")], method = "spearman"))
}

# 4A.2. ANOVA: PM2.5 AQI by Historic Redlining Categories (WITH CONTROLS)
pm25_anova_results <- run_redlining_anova(pm25_redlining, "aqi_pm25_corrected")

if (!is.null(pm25_anova_results)) {
  print(pm25_anova_results$summary)
  if (pm25_anova_results$significant) {
    cat("\nSignificant differences found. Post-hoc results:\n")
    print(pm25_anova_results$letters)
  }
  
  # Visualization
  pm25_labeled_letters <- pm25_anova_results$letters %>%
    mutate(group_label = case_when(
      group == "C" ~ "C: Declining",
      group == "D" ~ "D: Hazardous",
      group == "IC" ~ "IC: Industrial",
      group == "Not Graded" ~ "Not Graded",
      TRUE ~ as.character(group)
    ))
  
  pm25_redlining_violin <- create_redlining_violin_plot(
    data_labeled = pm25_redlining,
    letters_labeled = pm25_labeled_letters,
    pollutant_label = "PM2.5",
    y_label = "AQI PM2.5 (Corrected)"
  ) +
    annotate("text", x = -Inf, y = Inf, label = "(b)", hjust = -0.5, vjust = 1.5, size = 5, fontface = "bold") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, vjust = 1, face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "none",
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
  
  print(pm25_redlining_violin)
}

# 4A.3. GWR: PM2.5 AQI by Historic Redlining (WITH CONTROLS)
cat("\n4A.3. GWR: PM2.5 AQI by Historic Redlining (WITH CONTROLS)\n")
cat("===========================================================\n")

# Data validation and preparation
cat("Data validation and preparation:\n")

if (inherits(pm25_redlining, "data.table")) {
  pm25_redlining <- as.data.frame(pm25_redlining)
}

cat("Original PM2.5 data dimensions:", dim(pm25_redlining), "\n")
cat("Data quality check:\n")
cat("  - Total rows:", nrow(pm25_redlining), "\n")
cat("  - Missing longitude:", sum(is.na(pm25_redlining$longitude)), "\n")
cat("  - Missing latitude:", sum(is.na(pm25_redlining$latitude)), "\n")
cat("  - Missing aqi_pm25_corrected:", sum(is.na(pm25_redlining$aqi_pm25_corrected)), "\n")
cat("  - Missing housing_grade_numeric:", sum(is.na(pm25_redlining$housing_grade_numeric)), "\n")

# Create complete cases dataset
pm25_redlining_spatial <- pm25_redlining %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(
    longitude = as.numeric(longitude),
    latitude = as.numeric(latitude),
    aqi_pm25_corrected = as.numeric(aqi_pm25_corrected),
    housing_grade_numeric = as.numeric(housing_grade_numeric)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(longitude), !is.na(latitude),
    !is.na(aqi_pm25_corrected), !is.na(housing_grade_numeric),
    is.finite(longitude), is.finite(latitude),
    is.finite(aqi_pm25_corrected), is.finite(housing_grade_numeric),
    longitude >= -77.5, longitude <= -76.0,
    latitude >= 39.0, latitude <= 40.0,
    aqi_pm25_corrected >= 0, aqi_pm25_corrected <= 500
  ) %>%
  group_by(site, longitude, latitude) %>%
  summarise(
    aqi_pm25_corrected = mean(aqi_pm25_corrected, na.rm = TRUE),
    housing_grade_numeric = mean(housing_grade_numeric, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

cat("After filtering - dimensions:", dim(pm25_redlining_spatial), "\n")

min_obs_needed <- 30

if (nrow(pm25_redlining_spatial) >= min_obs_needed) {
  
  coord_summary <- pm25_redlining_spatial %>%
    summarise(
      lon_min = min(longitude), lon_max = max(longitude),
      lat_min = min(latitude), lat_max = max(latitude),
      lon_range = max(longitude) - min(longitude),
      lat_range = max(latitude) - min(latitude)
    )
  
  cat("Coordinate ranges:\n")
  print(coord_summary)
  
  if (coord_summary$lon_min > -77.5 && coord_summary$lon_max < -76.0 &&
      coord_summary$lat_min > 39.0 && coord_summary$lat_max < 40.0) {
    
    tryCatch({
      temp_df <- as.data.frame(pm25_redlining_spatial)
      
      cat("Pre-spatial conversion check:\n")
      cat("  - Rows:", nrow(temp_df), "\n")
      cat("  - Unique coordinates:", nrow(unique(temp_df[, c("longitude", "latitude")])), "\n")
      
      coordinates(temp_df) <- ~longitude + latitude
      proj4string(temp_df) <- CRS("+proj=longlat +datum=WGS84")
      
      pm25_redlining_spatial <- temp_df
      
      cat("Spatial data frame created successfully\n")
      cat("Number of spatial points:", nrow(pm25_redlining_spatial), "\n")
      
      bbox_result <- bbox(pm25_redlining_spatial)
      cat("Bounding box:\n")
      print(bbox_result)
      
    }, error = function(e) {
      cat("Error creating spatial data frame:", e$message, "\n")
      stop("Cannot proceed with GWR - spatial data frame creation failed")
    })
    
    tryCatch({
      cat("Attempting bandwidth selection...\n")
      
      bw_pm25_redlining <- NULL
      
      approaches_to_try <- list(
        list(approach = "CV", kernel = "gaussian", adaptive = TRUE),
        list(approach = "AIC", kernel = "gaussian", adaptive = TRUE),
        list(approach = "CV", kernel = "bisquare", adaptive = TRUE),
        list(approach = "AIC", kernel = "bisquare", adaptive = TRUE),
        list(approach = "AIC", kernel = "gaussian", adaptive = FALSE)
      )
      
      for (i in seq_along(approaches_to_try)) {
        approach_params <- approaches_to_try[[i]]
        cat("Trying approach", i, ":", approach_params$approach, "with", approach_params$kernel, "kernel\n")
        
        bw_result <- tryCatch({
          do.call(bw.gwr, c(
            list(formula = aqi_pm25_corrected ~ housing_grade_numeric,
                 data = pm25_redlining_spatial,
                 longlat = TRUE),
            approach_params
          ))
        }, error = function(e) {
          cat("  Failed:", e$message, "\n")
          NULL
        })
        
        if (!is.null(bw_result) && is.finite(bw_result) && bw_result > 0) {
          bw_pm25_redlining <- bw_result
          cat("  Success! Bandwidth:", bw_pm25_redlining, "\n")
          break
        }
      }
      
      n_points <- nrow(pm25_redlining_spatial)
      if (is.null(bw_pm25_redlining) || !is.finite(bw_pm25_redlining)) {
        bw_pm25_redlining <- max(30, round(0.3 * n_points))
        cat("Using default bandwidth:", bw_pm25_redlining, "\n")
      }
      
      if (bw_pm25_redlining < 10) {
        bw_pm25_redlining <- 10
        cat("Adjusted bandwidth to minimum value:", bw_pm25_redlining, "\n")
      } else if (bw_pm25_redlining > 0.8 * n_points) {
        bw_pm25_redlining <- round(0.8 * n_points)
        cat("Adjusted bandwidth to maximum value:", bw_pm25_redlining, "\n")
      }
      
      cat("Running GWR with bandwidth:", bw_pm25_redlining, "\n")
      
      gwr_pm25_redlining <- gwr.basic(
        aqi_pm25_corrected ~ housing_grade_numeric, 
        data = pm25_redlining_spatial,
        bw = bw_pm25_redlining,
        kernel = "gaussian",
        adaptive = TRUE,
        longlat = TRUE,
        F1 = TRUE,
        F2 = TRUE,
        F3 = TRUE
      )
      
      cat("GWR Results Summary:\n")
      cat("Bandwidth (adaptive):", bw_pm25_redlining, "\n")
      cat("Global R-squared:", round(gwr_pm25_redlining$GW.diagnostic$gwR2, 4), "\n")
      
      if (!is.null(gwr_pm25_redlining$SDF) && 
          !is.null(gwr_pm25_redlining$SDF$Local_R2)) {
        
        local_r2_range <- range(gwr_pm25_redlining$SDF$Local_R2, na.rm = TRUE)
        cat("Local R-squared range:", round(local_r2_range[1], 4), "to", round(local_r2_range[2], 4), "\n")
        
        gwr_pm25_coefs <- data.frame(
          site = pm25_redlining_spatial$site,
          longitude = coordinates(pm25_redlining_spatial)[,1],
          latitude = coordinates(pm25_redlining_spatial)[,2],
          redlining_coef = gwr_pm25_redlining$SDF$housing_grade_numeric,
          local_r2 = gwr_pm25_redlining$SDF$Local_R2
        )
        
        gwr_pm25_coefs <- gwr_pm25_coefs %>%
          filter(!is.na(redlining_coef), !is.na(local_r2))
        
        if (nrow(gwr_pm25_coefs) > 0) {
          cat("Historic redlining coefficient summary:\n")
          print(summary(gwr_pm25_coefs$redlining_coef))
          
          cat("Local R-squared summary:\n")
          print(summary(gwr_pm25_coefs$local_r2))
          
          positive_coefs <- sum(gwr_pm25_coefs$redlining_coef > 0, na.rm = TRUE)
          negative_coefs <- sum(gwr_pm25_coefs$redlining_coef < 0, na.rm = TRUE)
          
          cat("Coefficient interpretation:\n")
          cat("  - Locations with positive coefficients (worse redlining → higher PM2.5 AQI):", positive_coefs, "\n")
          cat("  - Locations with negative coefficients (worse redlining → lower PM2.5 AQI):", negative_coefs, "\n")
          
        } else {
          cat("No valid coefficients extracted from GWR results\n")
        }
        
      } else {
        cat("GWR completed but results structure is invalid\n")
      }
      
    }, error = function(e) {
      cat("GWR failed for PM2.5-redlining analysis:", e$message, "\n")
      
      cat("\n" , paste(rep("=", 50), collapse = ""), "\n")
      cat("FALLBACK ANALYSIS: Alternative Spatial Methods\n")
      cat(paste(rep("=", 50), collapse = ""), "\n")
      
      pm25_ols_data <- tryCatch({
        as.data.frame(pm25_redlining_spatial)
      }, error = function(e2) {
        pm25_redlining_spatial
      })
      
      cat("\n1. Global OLS Regression:\n")
      cat("-------------------------\n")
      pm25_ols_model <- lm(aqi_pm25_corrected ~ housing_grade_numeric, data = pm25_ols_data)
      print(summary(pm25_ols_model))
      
      if ("longitude" %in% names(pm25_ols_data) && "latitude" %in% names(pm25_ols_data)) {
        cat("\n2. Spatial Autocorrelation Test:\n")
        cat("--------------------------------\n")
        
        tryCatch({
          library(spdep)
          coords <- pm25_ols_data[, c("longitude", "latitude")]
          coords_unique <- unique(coords)
          
          if (nrow(coords_unique) > 3) {
            nb <- dnearneigh(as.matrix(coords_unique), 0, 0.1)
            
            if (any(card(nb) > 0)) {
              lw <- nb2listw(nb, style = "W", zero.policy = TRUE)
              moran_test <- moran.test(residuals(pm25_ols_model), lw, zero.policy = TRUE)
```
