```{r}
cat("=============================================================================\n")
cat("4F. WHEN DO DIFFERENT VULNERABILITY AREAS EXPERIENCE POOR AIR QUALITY?\n")
cat("(High, Moderate, and Low Vulnerability - After all controls)\n")
cat("(After controlling for population density and land use)\n")
cat("=============================================================================\n\n")

# Function to analyze vulnerability effects for a given pollutant
# Now includes High, Moderate, and Low vulnerability categories
# Updated to handle multiple years (Winter 2024-25 vs Winter 2025-26, etc.)
analyze_vulnerability_effects <- function(results_data, pollutant_name) {
  vulnerability_effects <- results_data %>%
    filter(model_fit == "Success") %>%
    mutate(
      month_name = month.name[month(date)],
      year = year(date),
      # Use the standardized helper for consistency
      season = get_season_year(date)
    )
  
  # Categorize vulnerability effects based on coefficient direction and significance
  # The coefficient coef_social_vuln represents: higher social vulnerability → effect on AQI
  # Positive coefficient = higher vulnerability associated with worse AQI
  # Negative coefficient = higher vulnerability associated with better AQI (or low vulnerability areas have worse AQI)
  
  # Categorize vulnerability effects into High, Moderate, and Low vulnerability categories
  # Calculate thresholds based on coefficient magnitude (for significant positive coefficients)
  sig_positive_coefs <- vulnerability_effects$coef_social_vuln[vulnerability_effects$coef_social_vuln_p < 0.05 & vulnerability_effects$coef_social_vuln > 0]
  if (length(sig_positive_coefs) > 0) {
    # Use tertiles to split positive significant coefficients into High and Moderate
    high_threshold <- quantile(sig_positive_coefs, 0.67, na.rm = TRUE)  # Top third
    moderate_threshold <- quantile(sig_positive_coefs, 0.33, na.rm = TRUE)  # Bottom third
  } else {
    # Default thresholds if no positive significant coefficients
    high_threshold <- 0.1
    moderate_threshold <- 0.05
  }
  
  vulnerability_effects <- vulnerability_effects %>%
    mutate(
      # HIGH VULNERABILITY WORSE: Large positive significant coefficient (top third)
      high_vuln_sig = coef_social_vuln_p < 0.05 & coef_social_vuln > high_threshold,
      
      # MODERATE VULNERABILITY WORSE: Small positive significant coefficient (bottom two-thirds)
      moderate_vuln_sig = coef_social_vuln_p < 0.05 & coef_social_vuln > 0 & coef_social_vuln <= high_threshold,
      
      # LOW VULNERABILITY WORSE: Negative significant coefficient
      low_vuln_sig = coef_social_vuln_p < 0.05 & coef_social_vuln < 0
    )
  
  # Overall summary
  overall_summary <- vulnerability_effects %>%
    summarise(
      total_days = n(),
      # High vulnerability category (large positive significant coefficient)
      days_high_vuln_sig = sum(high_vuln_sig, na.rm = TRUE),
      pct_high_vuln_sig = round(days_high_vuln_sig / total_days * 100, 1),
      # Moderate vulnerability category (small positive significant coefficient)
      days_moderate_vuln_sig = sum(moderate_vuln_sig, na.rm = TRUE),
      pct_moderate_vuln_sig = round(days_moderate_vuln_sig / total_days * 100, 1),
      # Low vulnerability category (negative significant coefficient)
      days_low_vuln_sig = sum(low_vuln_sig, na.rm = TRUE),
      pct_low_vuln_sig = round(days_low_vuln_sig / total_days * 100, 1)
    )
  
  # Seasonal analysis
  seasonal_summary <- vulnerability_effects %>%
    group_by(season) %>%
    summarise(
      total_days = n(),
      # High vulnerability category (large positive significant coefficient)
      days_high_vuln_sig = sum(high_vuln_sig, na.rm = TRUE),
      pct_high_vuln_sig = round(days_high_vuln_sig / total_days * 100, 1),
      # Moderate vulnerability category (small positive significant coefficient)
      days_moderate_vuln_sig = sum(moderate_vuln_sig, na.rm = TRUE),
      pct_moderate_vuln_sig = round(days_moderate_vuln_sig / total_days * 100, 1),
      # Low vulnerability category (negative significant coefficient)
      days_low_vuln_sig = sum(low_vuln_sig, na.rm = TRUE),
      pct_low_vuln_sig = round(days_low_vuln_sig / total_days * 100, 1),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    arrange(desc(pct_high_vuln_sig))
  
  # Monthly analysis
  monthly_summary <- vulnerability_effects %>%
    group_by(month_name) %>%
    summarise(
      total_days = n(),
      # High vulnerability category (large positive significant coefficient)
      days_high_vuln_sig = sum(high_vuln_sig, na.rm = TRUE),
      pct_high_vuln_sig = round(days_high_vuln_sig / total_days * 100, 1),
      # Moderate vulnerability category (small positive significant coefficient)
      days_moderate_vuln_sig = sum(moderate_vuln_sig, na.rm = TRUE),
      pct_moderate_vuln_sig = round(days_moderate_vuln_sig / total_days * 100, 1),
      # Low vulnerability category (negative significant coefficient)
      days_low_vuln_sig = sum(low_vuln_sig, na.rm = TRUE),
      pct_low_vuln_sig = round(days_low_vuln_sig / total_days * 100, 1),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    arrange(desc(pct_high_vuln_sig))
  
  return(list(
    overall = overall_summary,
    seasonal = seasonal_summary,
    monthly = monthly_summary,
    daily_data = vulnerability_effects
  ))
}

# ============================================================================
# ANALYSIS 1: PM2.5 CORRECTED
# ============================================================================
cat("\n")
cat("█████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████\n")
cat("█                    1. PM2.5 CORRECTED AQI                             █\n")
cat("█████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████\n\n")

pm25_results <- analyze_vulnerability_effects(results_by_day_pm25, "PM2.5")

cat("OVERALL SUMMARY - PM2.5:\n")
cat("========================\n")
cat("Total analysis days:", pm25_results$overall$total_days, "\n")
cat("\nInterpretation: The coefficient represents the effect of social vulnerability on AQI.\n")
cat("  High = large positive coefficient (significant) | Moderate = small positive coefficient (significant) | Low = negative coefficient (significant)\n\n")
cat("HIGH VULNERABILITY CATEGORY:\n")
cat("  Significant days:", pm25_results$overall$days_high_vuln_sig, "(", pm25_results$overall$pct_high_vuln_sig, "%)\n\n")
cat("MODERATE VULNERABILITY CATEGORY:\n")
cat("  Significant days:", pm25_results$overall$days_moderate_vuln_sig, "(", pm25_results$overall$pct_moderate_vuln_sig, "%)\n\n")
cat("LOW VULNERABILITY CATEGORY:\n")
cat("  Significant days:", pm25_results$overall$days_low_vuln_sig, "(", pm25_results$overall$pct_low_vuln_sig, "%)\n\n")

cat("SEASONAL BREAKDOWN - PM2.5:\n")
cat("===========================\n")
print(pm25_results$seasonal)

cat("\nMONTHLY BREAKDOWN - PM2.5:\n")
cat("==========================\n")
print(pm25_results$monthly)

# PM2.5: Graph showing percentage of days social vulnerability is significant by season
# Broken down by High, Moderate, and Low vulnerability categories
pm25_seasonal_long <- pm25_results$seasonal %>%
  dplyr::select(season, pct_high_vuln_sig, pct_moderate_vuln_sig, pct_low_vuln_sig) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(pct_high_vuln_sig, pct_moderate_vuln_sig, pct_low_vuln_sig),
               names_to = "vulnerability_category", values_to = "percentage") %>%
  mutate(
    vulnerability_category = case_when(
      vulnerability_category == "pct_high_vuln_sig"     ~ "High vulnerability worse",
      vulnerability_category == "pct_moderate_vuln_sig" ~ "Moderate vulnerability worse",
      vulnerability_category == "pct_low_vuln_sig"      ~ "Low vulnerability worse"
    ),
    vulnerability_category = factor(vulnerability_category,
                                    levels = c("High vulnerability worse",
                                               "Moderate vulnerability worse",
                                               "Low vulnerability worse")),
    # Retain the correct chronological ordering established by get_season_year
    season = factor(season, levels = c(
      "Winter 2024-2025", "Spring 2025", "Summer 2025", "Fall 2025",
      "Winter 2025-2026", "Spring 2026", "Summer 2026", "Fall 2026",
      "Winter 2026-2027"
    ))
  )

pm25_seasonal_plot <- ggplot(pm25_seasonal_long,
                             aes(x = percentage,
                                 y = season,
                                 fill = vulnerability_category)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(percentage, 1), "%")),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
            hjust = -0.1, size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "High vulnerability worse"     = "#D55E00",
      "Moderate vulnerability worse" = "#E69F00",
      "Low vulnerability worse"      = "#009E73"
    ),
    name = "Vulnerability Category"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Percentage of Days (%)",
    y = "Season"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, max(pm25_seasonal_long$percentage, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.25),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05)))

print(pm25_seasonal_plot)

# ============================================================================
# ANALYSIS 2: PM10 CORRECTED
# ============================================================================
cat("\n\n")
cat("█████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████\n")
cat("█                    2. PM10 CORRECTED AQI                              █\n")
cat("█████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████\n\n")

pm10_results <- analyze_vulnerability_effects(results_by_day_pm10, "PM10")

cat("OVERALL SUMMARY - PM10:\n")
cat("=======================\n")
cat("Total analysis days:", pm10_results$overall$total_days, "\n")
cat("\nInterpretation: The coefficient represents the effect of social vulnerability on AQI.\n")
cat("  High = large positive coefficient (significant) | Moderate = small positive coefficient (significant) | Low = negative coefficient (significant)\n\n")
cat("HIGH VULNERABILITY CATEGORY:\n")
cat("  Significant days:", pm10_results$overall$days_high_vuln_sig, "(", pm10_results$overall$pct_high_vuln_sig, "%)\n\n")
cat("MODERATE VULNERABILITY CATEGORY:\n")
cat("  Significant days:", pm10_results$overall$days_moderate_vuln_sig, "(", pm10_results$overall$pct_moderate_vuln_sig, "%)\n\n")
cat("LOW VULNERABILITY CATEGORY:\n")
cat("  Significant days:", pm10_results$overall$days_low_vuln_sig, "(", pm10_results$overall$pct_low_vuln_sig, "%)\n\n")

cat("SEASONAL BREAKDOWN - PM10:\n")
cat("==========================\n")
print(pm10_results$seasonal)

cat("\nMONTHLY BREAKDOWN - PM10:\n")
cat("=========================\n")
print(pm10_results$monthly)

# PM10: Graph showing percentage of days social vulnerability is significant by season
# Broken down by High, Moderate, and Low vulnerability categories
pm10_seasonal_long <- pm10_results$seasonal %>%
  dplyr::select(season, pct_high_vuln_sig, pct_moderate_vuln_sig, pct_low_vuln_sig) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(pct_high_vuln_sig, pct_moderate_vuln_sig, pct_low_vuln_sig),
               names_to = "vulnerability_category", values_to = "percentage") %>%
  mutate(
    vulnerability_category = case_when(
      vulnerability_category == "pct_high_vuln_sig"     ~ "High vulnerability worse",
      vulnerability_category == "pct_moderate_vuln_sig" ~ "Moderate vulnerability worse",
      vulnerability_category == "pct_low_vuln_sig"      ~ "Low vulnerability worse"
    ),
    vulnerability_category = factor(vulnerability_category,
                                    levels = c("High vulnerability worse",
                                               "Moderate vulnerability worse",
                                               "Low vulnerability worse")),
    # Retain the correct chronological ordering established by get_season_year
    season = factor(season, levels = c(
      "Winter 2024-2025", "Spring 2025", "Summer 2025", "Fall 2025",
      "Winter 2025-2026", "Spring 2026", "Summer 2026", "Fall 2026",
      "Winter 2026-2027"
    ))
  )

pm10_seasonal_plot <- ggplot(pm10_seasonal_long,
                             aes(x = percentage,
                                 y = season,
                                 fill = vulnerability_category)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(percentage, 1), "%")),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
            hjust = -0.1, size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "High vulnerability worse"     = "#D55E00",
      "Moderate vulnerability worse" = "#E69F00",
      "Low vulnerability worse"      = "#009E73"
    ),
    name = "Vulnerability Category"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Percentage of Days (%)",
    y = "Season"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, max(pm10_seasonal_long$percentage, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.25),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05)))

print(pm10_seasonal_plot)

# ============================================================================
# ANALYSIS 3: OZONE CORRECTED
# ============================================================================
cat("\n\n")
cat("█████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████\n")
cat("█                    3. OZONE CORRECTED AQI                             █\n")
cat("█████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████\n\n")

ozone_results <- analyze_vulnerability_effects(results_by_day_ozone, "Ozone")

cat("OVERALL SUMMARY - OZONE:\n")
cat("========================\n")
cat("Total analysis days:", ozone_results$overall$total_days, "\n")
cat("\nInterpretation: The coefficient represents the effect of social vulnerability on AQI.\n")
cat("  High = large positive coefficient (significant) | Moderate = small positive coefficient (significant) | Low = negative coefficient (significant)\n\n")
cat("HIGH VULNERABILITY CATEGORY:\n")
cat("  Significant days:", ozone_results$overall$days_high_vuln_sig, "(", ozone_results$overall$pct_high_vuln_sig, "%)\n\n")
cat("MODERATE VULNERABILITY CATEGORY:\n")
cat("  Significant days:", ozone_results$overall$days_moderate_vuln_sig, "(", ozone_results$overall$pct_moderate_vuln_sig, "%)\n\n")
cat("LOW VULNERABILITY CATEGORY:\n")
cat("  Significant days:", ozone_results$overall$days_low_vuln_sig, "(", ozone_results$overall$pct_low_vuln_sig, "%)\n\n")

cat("SEASONAL BREAKDOWN - OZONE:\n")
cat("===========================\n")
print(ozone_results$seasonal)

cat("\nMONTHLY BREAKDOWN - OZONE:\n")
cat("==========================\n")
print(ozone_results$monthly)

# Ozone: Graph showing percentage of days social vulnerability is significant by season
# Broken down by High, Moderate, and Low vulnerability categories
ozone_seasonal_long <- ozone_results$seasonal %>%
  dplyr::select(season, pct_high_vuln_sig, pct_moderate_vuln_sig, pct_low_vuln_sig) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(pct_high_vuln_sig, pct_moderate_vuln_sig, pct_low_vuln_sig),
               names_to = "vulnerability_category", values_to = "percentage") %>%
  mutate(
    vulnerability_category = case_when(
      vulnerability_category == "pct_high_vuln_sig"     ~ "High vulnerability worse",
      vulnerability_category == "pct_moderate_vuln_sig" ~ "Moderate vulnerability worse",
      vulnerability_category == "pct_low_vuln_sig"      ~ "Low vulnerability worse"
    ),
    vulnerability_category = factor(vulnerability_category,
                                    levels = c("High vulnerability worse",
                                               "Moderate vulnerability worse",
                                               "Low vulnerability worse")),
    # Retain the correct chronological ordering established by get_season_year
    season = factor(season, levels = c(
      "Winter 2024-2025", "Spring 2025", "Summer 2025", "Fall 2025",
      "Winter 2025-2026", "Spring 2026", "Summer 2026", "Fall 2026",
      "Winter 2026-2027"
    ))
  )

ozone_seasonal_plot <- ggplot(ozone_seasonal_long,
                              aes(x = percentage,
                                  y = season,
                                  fill = vulnerability_category)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(percentage, 1), "%")),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
            hjust = -0.1, size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "High vulnerability worse"     = "#D55E00",
      "Moderate vulnerability worse" = "#E69F00",
      "Low vulnerability worse"      = "#009E73"
    ),
    name = "Vulnerability Category"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Percentage of Days (%)",
    y = "Season"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, max(ozone_seasonal_long$percentage, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.25),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05)))

print(ozone_seasonal_plot)

# ============================================================================
# COMPARATIVE SUMMARY ACROSS ALL POLLUTANTS
# ============================================================================
cat("\n\n")
cat("█████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████\n")
cat("█                    COMPARATIVE SUMMARY                                █\n")
cat("█████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████\n\n")

# Create comparison table - shows High, Moderate, and Low vulnerability categories
comparison_table <- data.frame(
  Pollutant = c("PM2.5", "PM10", "Ozone"),
  Total_Days = c(pm25_results$overall$total_days, 
                 pm10_results$overall$total_days, 
                 ozone_results$overall$total_days),
  High_Vuln_Sig_Days = c(pm25_results$overall$days_high_vuln_sig,
                          pm10_results$overall$days_high_vuln_sig,
                          ozone_results$overall$days_high_vuln_sig),
  High_Vuln_Sig_Pct = c(pm25_results$overall$pct_high_vuln_sig,
                         pm10_results$overall$pct_high_vuln_sig,
                         ozone_results$overall$pct_high_vuln_sig),
  Moderate_Vuln_Sig_Days = c(pm25_results$overall$days_moderate_vuln_sig,
                              pm10_results$overall$days_moderate_vuln_sig,
                              ozone_results$overall$days_moderate_vuln_sig),
  Moderate_Vuln_Sig_Pct = c(pm25_results$overall$pct_moderate_vuln_sig,
                             pm10_results$overall$pct_moderate_vuln_sig,
                             ozone_results$overall$pct_moderate_vuln_sig),
  Low_Vuln_Sig_Days = c(pm25_results$overall$days_low_vuln_sig,
                         pm10_results$overall$days_low_vuln_sig,
                         ozone_results$overall$days_low_vuln_sig),
  Low_Vuln_Sig_Pct = c(pm25_results$overall$pct_low_vuln_sig,
                        pm10_results$overall$pct_low_vuln_sig,
                        ozone_results$overall$pct_low_vuln_sig)
)

cat("COMPARISON TABLE:\n")
cat("=================\n")
print(comparison_table, row.names = FALSE)

# ============================================================================
# SINGLE PUBLISHABLE GRAPHIC: High Vulnerability Worse vs Moderate and Low
# ============================================================================
cat("\n\n")
cat("█████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████\n")
cat("█              HIGH VULNERABILITY WORSE: COMPARISON ACROSS POLLUTANTS   █\n")
cat("█████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████\n\n")

# Prepare data showing percentage of days where each vulnerability category is worse
# Include seasonal breakdown
combined_seasonal <- bind_rows(
  pm25_results$seasonal %>% mutate(Pollutant = "PM2.5"),
  pm10_results$seasonal %>% mutate(Pollutant = "PM10"),
  ozone_results$seasonal %>% mutate(Pollutant = "Ozone")
)

# Extract date ranges for each season from daily data
# Season names now include years (e.g., "Winter 2024-2025", "Spring 2025")
season_date_ranges <- pm25_results$daily_data %>%
  group_by(season) %>%
  summarise(
    min_date = min(date, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_date = max(date, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_days = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Format dates: use abbreviated month names
    start_month = format(min_date, "%b"),
    start_year = format(min_date, "%Y"),
    end_month = format(max_date, "%b"),
    end_year = format(max_date, "%Y"),
    # Create formatted date strings
    start_date_str = paste0(start_month, ". ", start_year),
    end_date_str = paste0(end_month, ". ", end_year),
    # Create season label with date range
    # The season already has the year, so we add the specific month range
    season_label = paste0(season, "\n(", start_date_str, " - ", end_date_str, ")")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(season, season_label, n_days)

cat("\nSeason date ranges:\n")
print(season_date_ranges)

# Create lookup for season labels
season_labels <- setNames(season_date_ranges$season_label, season_date_ranges$season)

# Create ordered season levels based on chronological order
# Extract unique seasons and sort them chronologically
all_seasons <- unique(combined_seasonal$season)

# Parse season names to get year and season type for sorting
season_order <- tibble(season = all_seasons) %>%
  mutate(
    # Extract season type (Winter, Spring, Summer, Fall)
    season_type = str_extract(season, "^(Winter|Spring|Summer|Fall)"),
    # Extract year(s)
    year_part = str_extract(season, "\\d{4}"),
    # For Winter with format "Winter YYYY-YYYY", extract first year
    year_num = as.numeric(ifelse(str_detect(season, "\\d{4}-\\d{4}"),
                                  str_extract(season, "\\d{4}(?=-)"),
                                  year_part)),
    # Season type order within year
    season_type_order = case_when(
      season_type == "Winter" ~ 1,
      season_type == "Spring" ~ 2,
      season_type == "Summer" ~ 3,
      season_type == "Fall" ~ 4
    ),
    # Combined sort key
    sort_key = year_num * 10 + season_type_order
  ) %>%
  arrange(sort_key) %>%
  pull(season)

cat("Season order (chronological):\n")
print(season_order)

vulnerability_comparison <- combined_seasonal %>%
  dplyr::select(Pollutant, season, pct_high_vuln_sig, pct_moderate_vuln_sig, pct_low_vuln_sig,
                days_high_vuln_sig, days_moderate_vuln_sig, days_low_vuln_sig, total_days) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(pct_high_vuln_sig, pct_moderate_vuln_sig, pct_low_vuln_sig),
    names_to = "vulnerability_category",
    values_to = "percentage"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    vulnerability_category = case_when(
      vulnerability_category == "pct_high_vuln_sig" ~ "High",
      vulnerability_category == "pct_moderate_vuln_sig" ~ "Moderate",
      vulnerability_category == "pct_low_vuln_sig" ~ "Low"
    ),
    vulnerability_category = factor(vulnerability_category, levels = c("High", "Moderate", "Low")),
    Pollutant = factor(Pollutant, levels = c("PM2.5", "PM10", "Ozone")),
    season = factor(season, levels = season_order)
  )

# Calculate significance tests for differences between vulnerability categories within each season-pollutant combination
# Use chi-square test of proportions
cat("\n=== SIGNIFICANCE TESTS: Differences Between Vulnerability Categories Within Each Season ===\n")
significance_results <- vulnerability_comparison %>%
  group_by(Pollutant, season) %>%
  summarise(
    high_days = first(days_high_vuln_sig),
    moderate_days = first(days_moderate_vuln_sig),
    low_days = first(days_low_vuln_sig),
    total = first(total_days),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    # Create contingency table for chi-square test
    # Test if proportions differ significantly
    chi_sq_p = {
      # Create a matrix: success (significant days) vs failure (non-significant days) for each category
      # Note: Each category can have significant days, but they're mutually exclusive categories
      # So we test if the distribution of significant days across categories differs from expected
      observed <- c(high_days, moderate_days, low_days)
      # Expected: if all categories were equal, each would have (total/3) significant days
      # But actually, we want to test if the proportions differ
      # Use chi-square test for proportions
      if(sum(observed) > 0 && total > 0) {
        # Test if proportions are different
        # Create a 2x3 table: significant vs non-significant for each category
        sig_matrix <- matrix(c(
          high_days, total - high_days,
          moderate_days, total - moderate_days,
          low_days, total - low_days
        ), nrow = 3, ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE)
        
        # Chi-square test
        test_result <- tryCatch({
          chisq.test(sig_matrix)
        }, error = function(e) NULL)
        
        if(!is.null(test_result)) {
          test_result$p.value
        } else {
          NA_real_
        }
      } else {
        NA_real_
      }
    },
    is_significant = !is.na(chi_sq_p) && chi_sq_p < 0.05
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Print significance test results
for(i in 1:nrow(significance_results)) {
  poll <- significance_results$Pollutant[i]
  seas <- significance_results$season[i]
  p_val <- significance_results$chi_sq_p[i]
  sig <- significance_results$is_significant[i]
  
  cat("\n", poll, "-", seas, ":\n")
  if(!is.na(p_val)) {
    cat("  Chi-square test p-value:", format(p_val, digits = 4))
    if(sig) {
      cat(" * (significant differences between categories)\n")
    } else {
      cat(" (no significant differences)\n")
    }
  } else {
    cat("  Insufficient data for test\n")
  }
}

# Generate significance letters for each season-pollutant combination
# Use pairwise proportion tests when overall test is significant
significance_letters_list <- list()

for(i in 1:nrow(significance_results)) {
  poll <- as.character(significance_results$Pollutant[i])
  seas <- as.character(significance_results$season[i])
  
  if(significance_results$is_significant[i] && !is.na(significance_results$chi_sq_p[i])) {
    # Get the data for this combination
    high_days <- significance_results$high_days[i]
    moderate_days <- significance_results$moderate_days[i]
    low_days <- significance_results$low_days[i]
    total <- significance_results$total[i]
    
    # Perform pairwise proportion tests
    # High vs Moderate
    p_high_mod <- tryCatch({
      prop.test(c(high_days, moderate_days), c(total, total))$p.value
    }, error = function(e) NA_real_)
    
    # High vs Low
    p_high_low <- tryCatch({
      prop.test(c(high_days, low_days), c(total, total))$p.value
    }, error = function(e) NA_real_)
    
    # Moderate vs Low
    p_mod_low <- tryCatch({
      prop.test(c(moderate_days, low_days), c(total, total))$p.value
    }, error = function(e) NA_real_)
    
    # Assign letters based on pairwise comparisons
    # Groups that are NOT significantly different share the same letter
    letters <- c("a", "a", "a")  # Start with all same
    names(letters) <- c("High", "Moderate", "Low")
    
    # Adjust based on pairwise tests (using Bonferroni correction: p < 0.05/3 = 0.0167)
    if(!is.na(p_high_mod) && p_high_mod >= 0.0167) {
      # High and Moderate not different - keep same letter
    } else {
      # High and Moderate different - change Moderate
      letters["Moderate"] <- "b"
    }
    
    if(!is.na(p_high_low) && p_high_low >= 0.0167) {
      # High and Low not different
      if(letters["High"] == "a") {
        letters["Low"] <- "a"
      }
    } else {
      # High and Low different
      if(letters["Low"] == "a" && letters["Moderate"] == "b") {
        letters["Low"] <- "c"
      } else if(letters["Low"] == "a") {
        letters["Low"] <- "b"
      }
    }
    
    if(!is.na(p_mod_low) && p_mod_low >= 0.0167) {
      # Moderate and Low not different - make them same
      if(letters["Moderate"] == "b" && letters["Low"] == "c") {
        letters["Low"] <- "b"
      } else if(letters["Moderate"] == "a" && letters["Low"] == "b") {
        letters["Low"] <- "a"
      }
    }
    
    # Store letters
    key <- paste(poll, seas, sep = "_")
    significance_letters_list[[key]] <- letters
  } else {
    # No significant differences - all get same letter
    key <- paste(poll, seas, sep = "_")
    significance_letters_list[[key]] <- c("High" = "a", "Moderate" = "a", "Low" = "a")
  }
}

# Add significance letters to vulnerability_comparison
vulnerability_comparison <- vulnerability_comparison %>%
  mutate(
    key = paste(Pollutant, season, sep = "_"),
    sig_letter = map2_chr(key, vulnerability_category, function(k, cat) {
      if(k %in% names(significance_letters_list)) {
        significance_letters_list[[k]][as.character(cat)]
      } else {
        ""
      }
    })
  )

# ===========================================================================
# NATURE-STANDARD LOLLIPOP CHART — all seasons, including 2026 data
# Built with patchwork so every season present in the data gets its own panel
# ===========================================================================

# ── Global theme (set once, inherited by every panel) ──────────────────────
theme_nature <- theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    text               = element_text(family = "Helvetica", size = 12),
    axis.title         = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.text          = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),
    axis.ticks         = element_line(linewidth = 0.5),
    legend.text        = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.title       = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "#cccccc",
                                       linewidth = 0.4, linetype = "solid"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    plot.background    = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    panel.background   = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    strip.background   = element_blank(),
    strip.text         = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x        = element_markdown()   # allows PM<sub>2.5</sub>
  )
theme_set(theme_nature)

# ── Okabe-Ito palette (colorblind-safe) ────────────────────────────────────
vuln_colors <- c(
  "High vulnerability worse"     = "#D55E00",
  "Moderate vulnerability worse" = "#E69F00",
  "Low vulnerability worse"      = "#009E73"
)

# ── Reshape data with display labels ───────────────────────────────────────
vuln_long <- vulnerability_comparison %>%
  mutate(
    vuln_label = case_when(
      vulnerability_category == "High"     ~ "High vulnerability worse",
      vulnerability_category == "Moderate" ~ "Moderate vulnerability worse",
      vulnerability_category == "Low"      ~ "Low vulnerability worse"
    ),
    vuln_label = factor(vuln_label,
                        levels = c("High vulnerability worse",
                                   "Moderate vulnerability worse",
                                   "Low vulnerability worse")),
    # Add display-friendly season title: type only (year in subtitle)
    season_type = str_extract(as.character(season), "^(Winter|Spring|Summer|Fall)")
  )

# ── Determine chronological season order (already in season_order) ─────────
all_season_order <- season_order   # from the block above (~line 6265)

# ── Identify "zero panels" (every value == 0 for the season) ───────────────
season_is_zero <- vuln_long %>%
  group_by(season) %>%
  summarise(all_zero = all(percentage == 0, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# ── Utility: get subtitle date range for a season ──────────────────────────
get_season_subtitle <- function(seas) {
  row <- season_date_ranges %>% filter(as.character(season) == as.character(seas))
  if (nrow(row) == 0) return("")
  # Extract from season_label which already has the "(Mon. YYYY - Mon. YYYY)" part
  lbl <- row$season_label[1]
  # Season label format: "Season YYYY\n(Mon. YYYY - Mon. YYYY)"
  date_part <- str_extract(lbl, "\\(.*\\)")
  if (is.na(date_part)) return("")
  # Strip outer parentheses
  gsub("[()]", "", date_part)
}

# ── Compute top-2 outlier values (dynamically) for annotation ───────────────
# Only annotate if value is in the top 2 overall AND ≥ 15%
top_outliers <- vuln_long %>%
  arrange(desc(percentage)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 2) %>%
  filter(percentage >= 15)

# ── Panel builder function ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
make_season_panel <- function(seas, is_bottom_row, is_left_col) {
  d <- vuln_long %>% filter(as.character(season) == as.character(seas))
  zero_panel <- season_is_zero %>% 
    filter(as.character(season) == as.character(seas)) %>% 
    pull(all_zero)

  season_title    <- as.character(seas)
  season_subtitle <- get_season_subtitle(seas)

  # Base empty panel (keeps x-axis labels consistent even for zero panels)
  p <- ggplot(d, aes(x = Pollutant,
                     y = percentage,
                     color = vuln_label,
                     group = vuln_label)) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = c(
      "PM2.5" = "PM<sub>2.5</sub>",
      "PM10"  = "PM<sub>10</sub>",
      "Ozone" = "Ozone"
    )) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = vuln_colors,
      name   = "Vulnerability Category (Worse Air Quality)"
    ) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 35)) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = c(0, 10, 20, 30)) +
    labs(
      title    = season_title,
      subtitle = season_subtitle,
      x        = if (is_bottom_row) "Pollutant" else NULL,
      y        = NULL       # shared y-label applied via patchwork
    ) +
    theme(
      plot.title    = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold",   margin = margin(b = 1)),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 9, face = "plain", margin = margin(b = 4)),
      axis.title.x  = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "none"   # collected by patchwork
    )

  if (zero_panel) {
    # No lollipops — just a text annotation
    p <- p +
      annotate("text",
               x = 2, y = 17.5,
               label    = "No significant disparities detected",
               fontface = "italic",
               color    = "#888888",
               size     = 3.5)
  } else {
    p <- p +
      geom_segment(
        aes(xend = Pollutant, yend = 0),
        linewidth = 0.8,
        position  = position_dodge(width = 0.5)
      ) +
      geom_point(
        size     = 2.2,
        position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)
      )

    # Annotate top outliers that fall in this season
    outliers_here <- top_outliers %>%
      filter(as.character(season) == seas)
    if (nrow(outliers_here) > 0) {
      p <- p +
        geom_text(
          data = outliers_here,
          aes(label = paste0(round(percentage, 1), "%"), y = percentage + 1.2),
          size    = 3.2,
          position = position_dodge(width = 0.5),
          show.legend = FALSE
        )
    }
  }

  p
}

# ── Build one panel per season in chronological order ──────────────────────
n_seasons <- length(all_season_order)
# Determine grid layout: 2 columns, ceil(n/2) rows
n_cols <- 2
n_rows <- ceiling(n_seasons / n_cols)

panel_list <- lapply(seq_along(all_season_order), function(i) {
  seas       <- all_season_order[i]
  row_num    <- ceiling(i / n_cols)
  is_bottom  <- row_num == n_rows
  is_left    <- (i %% n_cols) == 1
  make_season_panel(seas, is_bottom, is_left)
})

# ── Combine with patchwork ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
combined_panels <- wrap_plots(panel_list, ncol = n_cols)

# ── Add shared y-axis label, panel letters, and collected legend ────────────
vulnerability_plot <- combined_panels +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") +
  plot_annotation(
    tag_levels = "a",
    tag_prefix = "(",
    tag_suffix = ")"
  ) &
  theme(
    legend.position   = "bottom",
    legend.direction  = "horizontal",
    legend.title      = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    legend.text       = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.box.margin = margin(t = 4),
    plot.tag          = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold")
  )

# Add a shared y-axis title on the far left using an empty spacer plot
y_label_plot <- ggplot() +
  annotate("text", x = 0, y = 0.5,
           label = "Days with Significant Air Quality Disparity (%)",
           angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 0.5,
           size = 4, fontface = "bold",
           family = "Helvetica") +
  theme_void()

final_plot <- y_label_plot + vulnerability_plot +
  plot_layout(widths = c(0.04, 1))

print(final_plot)

# ── Save outputs ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Height scales with number of panel rows (70mm per row) so all panels fit
fig_height_mm <- min(270, max(100, n_rows * 70))
cat("Figure dimensions: 180 x", fig_height_mm, "mm (", n_rows, "panel rows )\n")

tryCatch({
  ggsave("air_quality_disparities.pdf",
         plot   = final_plot,
         width  = 180, height = fig_height_mm, units = "mm",
         dpi    = 300, device = cairo_pdf)
  cat("Saved: air_quality_disparities.pdf\n")
}, error = function(e) {
  cat("PDF save failed (cairo_pdf unavailable?). Trying default device.\n")
  ggsave("air_quality_disparities.pdf",
         plot  = final_plot,
         width = 180, height = fig_height_mm, units = "mm", dpi = 300)
})

ggsave("air_quality_disparities.png",
       plot   = final_plot,
       width  = 180, height = fig_height_mm, units = "mm",
       dpi    = 300)
cat("Saved: air_quality_disparities.png\n")


# Key findings
# ============================================================================
# STATISTICAL SIGNIFICANCE TESTS FOR KEY FINDINGS
# ============================================================================
cat("\n\nSTATISTICAL TESTS FOR OVERALL PATTERNS:\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

# Test 1: Is the proportion of significant days greater than chance (5%)?
cat("1. Binomial Tests: Are significant days more frequent than random chance (5%)?\n")
cat("   (Testing if % sig days > 5% expected from Type I error)\n\n")

# PM2.5 binomial test (using High vulnerability category)
pm25_binom <- binom.test(
  pm25_results$overall$days_high_vuln_sig, 
  pm25_results$overall$total_days, 
  p = 0.05, 
  alternative = "greater"
)
cat("   PM2.5: p =", format.pval(pm25_binom$p.value, digits = 3), 
    ifelse(pm25_binom$p.value < 0.05, "***SIGNIFICANT***", "(not significant)"), "\n")

# PM10 binomial test (using High vulnerability category)
pm10_binom <- binom.test(
  pm10_results$overall$days_high_vuln_sig, 
  pm10_results$overall$total_days, 
  p = 0.05, 
  alternative = "greater"
)
cat("   PM10:  p =", format.pval(pm10_binom$p.value, digits = 3), 
    ifelse(pm10_binom$p.value < 0.05, "***SIGNIFICANT***", "(not significant)"), "\n")

# Ozone binomial test (using High vulnerability category)
ozone_binom <- binom.test(
  ozone_results$overall$days_high_vuln_sig, 
  ozone_results$overall$total_days, 
  p = 0.05, 
  alternative = "greater"
)
cat("   Ozone: p =", format.pval(ozone_binom$p.value, digits = 3), 
    ifelse(ozone_binom$p.value < 0.05, "***SIGNIFICANT***", "(not significant)"), "\n\n")

# Test 2: Are the daily coefficients significantly positive overall?
cat("2. One-Sample t-tests: Are social vulnerability coefficients significantly > 0?\n")
cat("   (Testing if average daily coefficient differs from zero)\n\n")

# PM2.5 t-test
pm25_ttest <- t.test(pm25_results$daily_data$coef_social_vuln, mu = 0, alternative = "greater")
cat("   PM2.5: Mean coef =", round(mean(pm25_results$daily_data$coef_social_vuln, na.rm = TRUE), 4),
    ", p =", format.pval(pm25_ttest$p.value, digits = 3),
    ifelse(pm25_ttest$p.value < 0.05, "***SIGNIFICANT***", "(not significant)"), "\n")

# PM10 t-test
pm10_ttest <- t.test(pm10_results$daily_data$coef_social_vuln, mu = 0, alternative = "greater")
cat("   PM10:  Mean coef =", round(mean(pm10_results$daily_data$coef_social_vuln, na.rm = TRUE), 4),
    ", p =", format.pval(pm10_ttest$p.value, digits = 3),
    ifelse(pm10_ttest$p.value < 0.05, "***SIGNIFICANT***", "(not significant)"), "\n")

# Ozone t-test
ozone_ttest <- t.test(ozone_results$daily_data$coef_social_vuln, mu = 0, alternative = "greater")
cat("   Ozone: Mean coef =", round(mean(ozone_results$daily_data$coef_social_vuln, na.rm = TRUE), 4),
    ", p =", format.pval(ozone_ttest$p.value, digits = 3),
    ifelse(ozone_ttest$p.value < 0.05, "***SIGNIFICANT***", "(not significant)"), "\n\n")

# Test 3: Chi-square test for seasonal differences
cat("3. Chi-Square Tests: Do significant days vary by season?\n\n")

# PM2.5 seasonal chi-square (using High vulnerability category)
pm25_seasonal_table <- table(
  Season = pm25_results$daily_data$season,
  Significant = pm25_results$daily_data$high_vuln_sig
)
pm25_chisq <- chisq.test(pm25_seasonal_table)
cat("   PM2.5: χ² =", round(pm25_chisq$statistic, 2), 
    ", p =", format.pval(pm25_chisq$p.value, digits = 3),
    ifelse(pm25_chisq$p.value < 0.05, "***SIGNIFICANT***", "(not significant)"), "\n")

# PM10 seasonal chi-square (using High vulnerability category)
pm10_seasonal_table <- table(
  Season = pm10_results$daily_data$season,
  Significant = pm10_results$daily_data$high_vuln_sig
)
pm10_chisq <- chisq.test(pm10_seasonal_table)
cat("   PM10:  χ² =", round(pm10_chisq$statistic, 2), 
    ", p =", format.pval(pm10_chisq$p.value, digits = 3),
    ifelse(pm10_chisq$p.value < 0.05, "***SIGNIFICANT***", "(not significant)"), "\n")

# Ozone seasonal chi-square (using High vulnerability category)
ozone_seasonal_table <- table(
  Season = ozone_results$daily_data$season,
  Significant = ozone_results$daily_data$high_vuln_sig
)
ozone_chisq <- chisq.test(ozone_seasonal_table)
cat("   Ozone: χ² =", round(ozone_chisq$statistic, 2), 
    ", p =", format.pval(ozone_chisq$p.value, digits = 3),
    ifelse(ozone_chisq$p.value < 0.05, "***SIGNIFICANT***", "(not significant)"), "\n\n")

cat("=============================================================================\n\n")

cat("KEY FINDINGS:\n")
cat("=============\n")
cat("1. PM2.5: High vulnerability category (large positive coefficient) is significant on", 
    pm25_results$overall$pct_high_vuln_sig, "% of analysis days\n")
cat("   (Binomial test: p =", format.pval(pm25_binom$p.value, digits = 3), ")\n")
cat("   - Worst season:", pm25_results$seasonal$season[1], "(", pm25_results$seasonal$pct_high_vuln_sig[1], "% of days)\n")
cat("   - Worst month:", pm25_results$monthly$month_name[1], "(", pm25_results$monthly$pct_high_vuln_sig[1], "% of days)\n")
cat("   - Overall mean coefficient:", round(mean(pm25_results$daily_data$coef_social_vuln, na.rm = TRUE), 4), 
    "(t-test: p =", format.pval(pm25_ttest$p.value, digits = 3), ")\n\n")

cat("2. PM10: High vulnerability category (large positive coefficient) is significant on", 
    pm10_results$overall$pct_high_vuln_sig, "% of analysis days\n")
cat("   (Binomial test: p =", format.pval(pm10_binom$p.value, digits = 3), ")\n")
cat("   - Worst season:", pm10_results$seasonal$season[1], "(", pm10_results$seasonal$pct_high_vuln_sig[1], "% of days)\n")
cat("   - Worst month:", pm10_results$monthly$month_name[1], "(", pm10_results$monthly$pct_high_vuln_sig[1], "% of days)\n")
cat("   - Overall mean coefficient:", round(mean(pm10_results$daily_data$coef_social_vuln, na.rm = TRUE), 4), 
    "(t-test: p =", format.pval(pm10_ttest$p.value, digits = 3), ")\n\n")

cat("3. Ozone: High vulnerability category (large positive coefficient) is significant on", 
    ozone_results$overall$pct_high_vuln_sig, "% of analysis days\n")
cat("   (Binomial test: p =", format.pval(ozone_binom$p.value, digits = 3), ")\n")
cat("   - Worst season:", ozone_results$seasonal$season[1], "(", ozone_results$seasonal$pct_high_vuln_sig[1], "% of days)\n")
cat("   - Worst month:", ozone_results$monthly$month_name[1], "(", ozone_results$monthly$pct_high_vuln_sig[1], "% of days)\n")
cat("   - Overall mean coefficient:", round(mean(ozone_results$daily_data$coef_social_vuln, na.rm = TRUE), 4), 
    "(t-test: p =", format.pval(ozone_ttest$p.value, digits = 3), ")\n\n")

cat("4. Overall Pattern: High vulnerability areas are disproportionately affected by poor air quality,\n")
cat("   with effects varying by pollutant type and season even after controlling for all spatial factors.\n")
cat("   Seasonal variation is statistically significant for all three pollutants.\n")
```

#Worst month air quality and disparities by social vulnerability
```{r}
# Analyze worst month per pollutant and disparities across social vulnerability categories

# ============================================================================
# CONFIGURATION: Worst Month Selection Method
# ============================================================================
# Set to TRUE to only consider months where ALL 3 vulnerability categories have data
# This ensures fair comparisons but may miss the truly worst months if some sites
# weren't operational during those periods
REQUIRE_COMPLETE_VULN_COVERAGE <- TRUE  # <-- CHANGE THIS TO FALSE to use all data

cat("=== WORST MONTH ANALYSIS CONFIGURATION ===\n")
cat("REQUIRE_COMPLETE_VULN_COVERAGE:", REQUIRE_COMPLETE_VULN_COVERAGE, "\n")
if(REQUIRE_COMPLETE_VULN_COVERAGE) {
  cat("  → Only months with data from ALL 3 vulnerability categories will be considered\n")
  cat("  → This ensures fair comparisons across vulnerability groups\n\n")
} else {
  cat("  → All months with data will be considered (some vulnerability categories may be missing)\n\n")
}

# Prepare data with temporal components and social vulnerability
worst_month_data <- daily_avg %>%
  filter(!is.na(timestamp_day) & !is.na(social_vuln_category)) %>%
  mutate(
    # Extract temporal components
    year = year(timestamp_day),
    month = month(timestamp_day),
    month_name = month.name[month],
    month_name = factor(month_name, levels = month.name),
    season = get_season_year(timestamp_day),
    
    # Ensure social_vuln_category is properly ordered
    social_vuln_category = factor(social_vuln_category, levels = c("High", "Moderate", "Low"))
  )

# ============================================================================
# DIAGNOSTIC: DATA AVAILABILITY BY MONTH AND VULNERABILITY CATEGORY
# ============================================================================
cat("=== DATA AVAILABILITY DIAGNOSTIC ===\n\n")

# Check PM2.5 data availability by month and vulnerability category
pm25_availability <- worst_month_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(aqi_pm25_corrected)) %>%
  group_by(month, month_name, social_vuln_category) %>%
  summarise(
    n_days = n(),
    n_sites = n_distinct(site),
    sites = paste(unique(site), collapse = ", "),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  arrange(month, social_vuln_category)

cat("PM2.5 Data Availability by Month and Vulnerability Category:\n")
print(pm25_availability %>% dplyr::select(month_name, social_vuln_category, n_days, n_sites))

# Check which vulnerability categories have data in each month
vuln_coverage <- worst_month_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(aqi_pm25_corrected)) %>%
  group_by(month, month_name) %>%
  summarise(
    has_high = "High" %in% social_vuln_category,
    has_moderate = "Moderate" %in% social_vuln_category,
    has_low = "Low" %in% social_vuln_category,
    n_vuln_categories = n_distinct(social_vuln_category),
    total_days = n(),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  arrange(month)

cat("\n\nMonths with complete vulnerability coverage (all 3 categories):\n")
complete_months <- vuln_coverage %>% filter(n_vuln_categories == 3)
print(complete_months)

# Check site operational dates
site_date_ranges <- worst_month_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(aqi_pm25_corrected)) %>%
  group_by(site, site_name, social_vuln_category) %>%
  summarise(
    first_date = min(timestamp_day),
    last_date = max(timestamp_day),
    n_days = n(),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  arrange(social_vuln_category, first_date)

cat("\n\nSite Operational Dates (PM2.5):\n")
print(site_date_ranges)

cat("\n⚠️  NOTE: If Low vulnerability sites started collecting data AFTER January,\n")
cat("   they won't appear in the January analysis. Consider using only months\n")
cat("   where ALL vulnerability categories have data for fair comparison.\n\n")

# ============================================================================
# 1. Identify worst month for each pollutant (based on average AQI)
# ============================================================================
cat("=== IDENTIFYING WORST MONTH PER POLLUTANT ===\n")

# Show ALL months ranked by mean AQI (not just the worst)
cat("\nPM2.5 Monthly Rankings (all months):\n")
pm25_monthly_avg <- worst_month_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(aqi_pm25_corrected)) %>%
  group_by(month, month_name) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_aqi = mean(aqi_pm25_corrected, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_aqi = median(aqi_pm25_corrected, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_days = n(),
    n_sites = n_distinct(site),
    n_vuln_cats = n_distinct(social_vuln_category),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_aqi))

print(pm25_monthly_avg)

# Determine worst month based on configuration
if(REQUIRE_COMPLETE_VULN_COVERAGE && nrow(complete_months) > 0) {
  # Use only months with complete vulnerability coverage
  pm25_monthly_filtered <- worst_month_data %>%
    filter(!is.na(aqi_pm25_corrected)) %>%
    filter(month %in% complete_months$month) %>%
    group_by(month, month_name) %>%
    summarise(
      mean_aqi = mean(aqi_pm25_corrected, na.rm = TRUE),
      median_aqi = median(aqi_pm25_corrected, na.rm = TRUE),
      n_days = n(),
      n_vuln_cats = n_distinct(social_vuln_category),
      .groups = 'drop'
    ) %>%
    arrange(desc(mean_aqi))
  
  worst_month_pm25 <- pm25_monthly_filtered[1, ]
  cat("\n✓ PM2.5 worst month (complete vuln coverage):", as.character(worst_month_pm25$month_name), 
      "(Mean AQI:", round(worst_month_pm25$mean_aqi, 1), ")\n")
  
  # Also show what would have been selected without the filter
  worst_month_pm25_unfiltered <- pm25_monthly_avg[1, ]
  if(as.character(worst_month_pm25_unfiltered$month_name) != as.character(worst_month_pm25$month_name)) {
    cat("  ⚠️ Note: Without complete coverage requirement, worst month would be:", 
        as.character(worst_month_pm25_unfiltered$month_name),
        "(Mean AQI:", round(worst_month_pm25_unfiltered$mean_aqi, 1), 
        ", but only", worst_month_pm25_unfiltered$n_vuln_cats, "vuln categories had data)\n")
  }
} else {
  # Use all data (original behavior)
  worst_month_pm25 <- pm25_monthly_avg[1, ]
  cat("\nPM2.5 worst month:", as.character(worst_month_pm25$month_name), 
      "(Mean AQI:", round(worst_month_pm25$mean_aqi, 1), 
      ", n_days:", worst_month_pm25$n_days,
      ", n_vuln_cats:", worst_month_pm25$n_vuln_cats, ")\n")
  
  if(worst_month_pm25$n_vuln_cats < 3) {
    cat("  ⚠️ WARNING: Only", worst_month_pm25$n_vuln_cats, 
        "vulnerability categories have data in this month!\n")
    cat("  Set REQUIRE_COMPLETE_VULN_COVERAGE <- TRUE for fair comparisons.\n")
  }
}

# PM10 worst month - check complete coverage for PM10 specifically
pm10_vuln_coverage <- worst_month_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(aqi_pm10_corrected)) %>%
  group_by(month, month_name) %>%
  summarise(n_vuln_cats = n_distinct(social_vuln_category), .groups = 'drop')

pm10_complete_months <- pm10_vuln_coverage %>% filter(n_vuln_cats == 3)

pm10_monthly_avg <- worst_month_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(aqi_pm10_corrected)) %>%
  group_by(month, month_name) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_aqi = mean(aqi_pm10_corrected, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_aqi = median(aqi_pm10_corrected, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_days = n(),
    n_vuln_cats = n_distinct(social_vuln_category),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_aqi))

if(REQUIRE_COMPLETE_VULN_COVERAGE && nrow(pm10_complete_months) > 0) {
  pm10_monthly_filtered <- pm10_monthly_avg %>% filter(month %in% pm10_complete_months$month)
  worst_month_pm10 <- pm10_monthly_filtered[1, ]
  cat("✓ PM10 worst month (complete vuln coverage):", as.character(worst_month_pm10$month_name), 
      "(Mean AQI:", round(worst_month_pm10$mean_aqi, 1), ")\n")
} else {
  worst_month_pm10 <- pm10_monthly_avg[1, ]
  cat("PM10 worst month:", as.character(worst_month_pm10$month_name), 
      "(Mean AQI:", round(worst_month_pm10$mean_aqi, 1), ")\n")
}

# Ozone worst month - check complete coverage for Ozone specifically
ozone_vuln_coverage <- worst_month_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(aqi_ozone_corrected)) %>%
  group_by(month, month_name) %>%
  summarise(n_vuln_cats = n_distinct(social_vuln_category), .groups = 'drop')

ozone_complete_months <- ozone_vuln_coverage %>% filter(n_vuln_cats == 3)

ozone_monthly_avg <- worst_month_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(aqi_ozone_corrected)) %>%
  group_by(month, month_name) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_aqi = mean(aqi_ozone_corrected, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_aqi = median(aqi_ozone_corrected, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_days = n(),
    n_vuln_cats = n_distinct(social_vuln_category),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_aqi))

if(REQUIRE_COMPLETE_VULN_COVERAGE && nrow(ozone_complete_months) > 0) {
  ozone_monthly_filtered <- ozone_monthly_avg %>% filter(month %in% ozone_complete_months$month)
  worst_month_ozone <- ozone_monthly_filtered[1, ]
  cat("✓ Ozone worst month (complete vuln coverage):", as.character(worst_month_ozone$month_name), 
      "(Mean AQI:", round(worst_month_ozone$mean_aqi, 1), ")\n\n")
} else {
  worst_month_ozone <- ozone_monthly_avg[1, ]
  cat("Ozone worst month:", as.character(worst_month_ozone$month_name), 
      "(Mean AQI:", round(worst_month_ozone$mean_aqi, 1), ")\n\n")
}

# 2. Analyze disparities by social vulnerability category for worst month of each pollutant
cat("=== DISPARITIES BY SOCIAL VULNERABILITY IN WORST MONTHS ===\n\n")

# PM2.5 disparities in worst month
pm25_worst_month_data <- worst_month_data %>%
  filter(month == worst_month_pm25$month & !is.na(aqi_pm25_corrected))

pm25_vuln_summary <- pm25_worst_month_data %>%
  group_by(social_vuln_category) %>%
  summarise(
    n_days = n(),
    n_sites = n_distinct(site),
    mean_aqi = mean(aqi_pm25_corrected, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_aqi = median(aqi_pm25_corrected, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_aqi = sd(aqi_pm25_corrected, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_aqi = min(aqi_pm25_corrected, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_aqi = max(aqi_pm25_corrected, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_poor = round(100 * sum(aqi_pm25_corrected > 100, na.rm = TRUE) / n(), 1),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )
# Calculate low mean AQI for reference
pm25_low_mean <- pm25_vuln_summary$mean_aqi[pm25_vuln_summary$social_vuln_category == "Low"]
if(length(pm25_low_mean) == 0) pm25_low_mean <- NA_real_
pm25_vuln_summary <- pm25_vuln_summary %>%
  mutate(
    diff_from_low = mean_aqi - pm25_low_mean,
    pct_diff_from_low = ifelse(!is.na(pm25_low_mean) & pm25_low_mean > 0,
                               round(100 * (mean_aqi - pm25_low_mean) / pm25_low_mean, 1),
                               NA_real_)
  )

cat("PM2.5 disparities in", as.character(worst_month_pm25$month_name), ":\n")
print(pm25_vuln_summary)
cat("\n")

# PM10 disparities in worst month
pm10_worst_month_data <- worst_month_data %>%
  filter(month == worst_month_pm10$month & !is.na(aqi_pm10_corrected))

pm10_vuln_summary <- pm10_worst_month_data %>%
  group_by(social_vuln_category) %>%
  summarise(
    n_days = n(),
    n_sites = n_distinct(site),
    mean_aqi = mean(aqi_pm10_corrected, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_aqi = median(aqi_pm10_corrected, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_aqi = sd(aqi_pm10_corrected, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_aqi = min(aqi_pm10_corrected, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_aqi = max(aqi_pm10_corrected, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_poor = round(100 * sum(aqi_pm10_corrected > 100, na.rm = TRUE) / n(), 1),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )
# Calculate low mean AQI for reference
pm10_low_mean <- pm10_vuln_summary$mean_aqi[pm10_vuln_summary$social_vuln_category == "Low"]
if(length(pm10_low_mean) == 0) pm10_low_mean <- NA_real_
pm10_vuln_summary <- pm10_vuln_summary %>%
  mutate(
    diff_from_low = mean_aqi - pm10_low_mean,
    pct_diff_from_low = ifelse(!is.na(pm10_low_mean) & pm10_low_mean > 0,
                               round(100 * (mean_aqi - pm10_low_mean) / pm10_low_mean, 1),
                               NA_real_)
  )

cat("PM10 disparities in", as.character(worst_month_pm10$month_name), ":\n")
print(pm10_vuln_summary)
cat("\n")

# Ozone disparities in worst month
ozone_worst_month_data <- worst_month_data %>%
  filter(month == worst_month_ozone$month & !is.na(aqi_ozone_corrected))

ozone_vuln_summary <- ozone_worst_month_data %>%
  group_by(social_vuln_category) %>%
  summarise(
    n_days = n(),
    n_sites = n_distinct(site),
    mean_aqi = mean(aqi_ozone_corrected, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_aqi = median(aqi_ozone_corrected, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_aqi = sd(aqi_ozone_corrected, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_aqi = min(aqi_ozone_corrected, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_aqi = max(aqi_ozone_corrected, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_poor = round(100 * sum(aqi_ozone_corrected > 100, na.rm = TRUE) / n(), 1),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )
# Calculate low mean AQI for reference
ozone_low_mean <- ozone_vuln_summary$mean_aqi[ozone_vuln_summary$social_vuln_category == "Low"]
if(length(ozone_low_mean) == 0) ozone_low_mean <- NA_real_
ozone_vuln_summary <- ozone_vuln_summary %>%
  mutate(
    diff_from_low = mean_aqi - ozone_low_mean,
    pct_diff_from_low = ifelse(!is.na(ozone_low_mean) & ozone_low_mean > 0,
                               round(100 * (mean_aqi - ozone_low_mean) / ozone_low_mean, 1),
                               NA_real_)
  )

cat("Ozone disparities in", as.character(worst_month_ozone$month_name), ":\n")
print(ozone_vuln_summary)
cat("\n")

# 3. Statistical tests for disparities
cat("=== STATISTICAL TESTS FOR DISPARITIES ===\n")

# Function to extract significance letters from Tukey HSD results
get_significance_letters <- function(tukey_result, categories) {
  # Try to use multcompView package if available, otherwise use manual method
  if(requireNamespace("multcompView", quietly = TRUE)) {
    tukey_df <- as.data.frame(tukey_result$social_vuln_category)
    p_vals <- tukey_df$`p adj`
    names(p_vals) <- rownames(tukey_df)
    
    # Convert to compact letter display
    tryCatch({
      letters_result <- multcompView::multcompLetters(p_vals)
      # Convert to named vector matching categories
      result <- rep("", length(categories))
      names(result) <- categories
      for(cat in categories) {
        result[cat] <- letters_result$Letters[cat]
      }
      return(result)
    }, error = function(e) {
      # Fall back to manual method
    })
  }
  
  # Manual method: Extract p-values from Tukey HSD
  tukey_df <- as.data.frame(tukey_result$social_vuln_category)
  
  # Create a matrix of p-values for all pairwise comparisons
  n_cats <- length(categories)
  p_matrix <- matrix(1, nrow = n_cats, ncol = n_cats)
  rownames(p_matrix) <- categories
  colnames(p_matrix) <- categories
  
  # Fill in p-values from Tukey results
  for(i in seq_len(nrow(tukey_df))) {
    # Try contrast column first (emmeans format), then rownames
    if("contrast" %in% names(tukey_df)) {
      comparison <- as.character(tukey_df$contrast[i])
    } else {
      comparison <- rownames(tukey_df)[i]
    }
    
    # Handle different formats: "High-Low", "High - Low", etc.
    comparison_clean <- trimws(comparison)
    # Try splitting on " - " first (emmeans format), then "-", then ":"
    if(grepl(" - ", comparison_clean)) {
      parts <- strsplit(comparison_clean, " - ")[[1]]
    } else if(grepl("-", comparison_clean)) {
      parts <- strsplit(comparison_clean, "-")[[1]]
    } else if(grepl(":", comparison_clean)) {
      parts <- strsplit(comparison_clean, ":")[[1]]
    } else {
      parts <- NULL
    }
    
    if(!is.null(parts) && length(parts) >= 2) {
      cat1 <- trimws(parts[1])
      cat2 <- trimws(parts[length(parts)])
      # Get p-value - try different column names
      if("p adj" %in% names(tukey_df)) {
        p_val <- tukey_df$`p adj`[i]
      } else if("p.value" %in% names(tukey_df)) {
        p_val <- tukey_df$p.value[i]
      } else if("p" %in% names(tukey_df)) {
        p_val <- tukey_df$p[i]
      } else {
        p_val <- 1
      }
      
      # Handle NA p-values
      if(is.na(p_val)) p_val <- 1
      
      if(cat1 %in% categories && cat2 %in% categories) {
        p_matrix[cat1, cat2] <- p_val
        p_matrix[cat2, cat1] <- p_val
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Assign letters using compact letter display
  # Groups that are NOT significantly different (p > 0.05) share letters
  # Start with all groups getting unique letters
  letters_result <- LETTERS[1:n_cats]
  names(letters_result) <- categories
  
  # Build equivalence groups: groups that are not significantly different share letters
  # Use a simple transitive closure approach
  for(iter in 1:n_cats) {  # Multiple passes to ensure transitive closure
    for(i in 1:(n_cats-1)) {
      for(j in (i+1):n_cats) {
        cat1 <- categories[i]
        cat2 <- categories[j]
        # If not significantly different, they should share the same letter
        if(p_matrix[cat1, cat2] > 0.05) {
          # Use the earlier letter (alphabetically)
          if(which(LETTERS == letters_result[cat1]) > which(LETTERS == letters_result[cat2])) {
            letters_result[cat1] <- letters_result[cat2]
          } else {
            letters_result[cat2] <- letters_result[cat1]
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Simplify to sequential letters starting from A
  unique_letters <- sort(unique(letters_result))
  letter_map <- setNames(LETTERS[1:length(unique_letters)], unique_letters)
  for(i in seq_along(letters_result)) {
    letters_result[i] <- letter_map[letters_result[i]]
  }
  
  return(letters_result)
}

# PM2.5 ANCOVA (controlling for covariates)
pm25_tukey <- NULL
pm25_sig_letters <- NULL
pm25_emmeans <- NULL
if(nrow(pm25_worst_month_data) > 0) {
  # Prepare data - ensure covariates are available and not NA
  pm25_model_data <- pm25_worst_month_data %>%
    filter(!is.na(aqi_pm25_corrected) & 
           !is.na(population_density) & 
           !is.na(land_use_type) & 
           !is.na(social_vuln_category))
  
  if(nrow(pm25_model_data) > 0) {
    # Fit ANCOVA model controlling for covariates (if factors have 2+ levels)
    sv_levels <- n_distinct(pm25_model_data$social_vuln_category)
    lu_levels <- n_distinct(pm25_model_data$land_use_type)
    
    if (sv_levels >= 2 && lu_levels >= 2) {
      pm25_ancova <- lm(aqi_pm25_corrected ~ social_vuln_category + 
                        population_density + land_use_type, 
                        data = pm25_model_data)
      pm25_ancova_summary <- summary(pm25_ancova)
      pm25_anova_table <- anova(pm25_ancova)
      
      cat("PM2.5 ANCOVA (", as.character(worst_month_pm25$month_name), ") - Controlling for population_density, land_use_type:\n")
      print(pm25_anova_table)
      cat("\nModel coefficients:\n")
      print(pm25_ancova_summary$coefficients)
      
      # Use emmeans for adjusted means and pairwise comparisons
      if(requireNamespace("emmeans", quietly = TRUE)) {
        pm25_emmeans <- emmeans::emmeans(pm25_ancova, ~ social_vuln_category)
      pm25_pairs <- pairs(pm25_emmeans, adjust = "tukey")
      
      cat("\nPM2.5 Adjusted means (controlling for covariates):\n")
      print(pm25_emmeans)
      
      cat("\nPM2.5 Pairwise comparisons (Tukey HSD, adjusted for covariates):\n")
      print(pm25_pairs)
      
      # Extract p-values for significance letters
      pairs_df <- as.data.frame(pm25_pairs)
      
      # Handle non-estimable comparisons (remove rows with nonEst or NA p-values)
      if("p.value" %in% names(pairs_df)) {
        # Convert character "nonEst" to NA
        if(is.character(pairs_df$p.value)) {
          pairs_df$p.value[pairs_df$p.value == "nonEst"] <- NA
        }
        # Convert to numeric, handling any remaining character values
        pairs_df$p.value <- as.numeric(pairs_df$p.value)
      }
      
      # Note about overall vs pairwise significance
      # Safely extract ANOVA p-value for social_vuln_category (Df == 2)
      anova_p_value <- tryCatch({
        pval <- pm25_anova_table[pm25_anova_table$Df == 2, "Pr(>F)"]
        if(length(pval) == 0) {
          NA_real_
        } else if(length(pval) > 1) {
          pval[1]  # Take first if multiple matches
        } else {
          as.numeric(pval)
        }
      }, error = function(e) {
        NA_real_
      })
      
      # Check if we have valid p-values for comparison
      has_valid_pairwise <- any(!is.na(pairs_df$p.value)) && any(pairs_df$p.value < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)
      has_valid_anova <- !is.na(anova_p_value) && is.finite(anova_p_value)
      
      if(has_valid_anova && anova_p_value < 0.05 && !has_valid_pairwise) {
        cat("\nNOTE: Overall ANCOVA shows significant effect of social vulnerability (p =", 
            round(anova_p_value, 4), 
            "), but no pairwise comparisons are significant after Tukey adjustment.\n")
        cat("This can occur when the effect is distributed across groups rather than concentrated in specific pairs,\n")
        cat("or when multiple testing correction makes pairwise tests more conservative.\n")
      }
      # Create a Tukey-like structure for the significance letter function
      pm25_tukey <- list(social_vuln_category = pairs_df)
      rownames(pm25_tukey$social_vuln_category) <- as.character(pairs_df$contrast)
      # Ensure p adj column exists
      if("p.value" %in% names(pairs_df)) {
        pm25_tukey$social_vuln_category$`p adj` <- pairs_df$p.value
      } else {
        pm25_tukey$social_vuln_category$`p adj` <- rep(1, nrow(pairs_df))
      }
      
      # Get significance letters - only if there are significant differences
      if(any(pairs_df$p.value < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)) {
        pm25_sig_letters <- get_significance_letters(pm25_tukey, levels(pm25_model_data$social_vuln_category))
        # Check if all letters are the same - if so, no significant differences to display
        if(length(unique(pm25_sig_letters)) == 1) {
          pm25_sig_letters <- NULL
        }
      } else {
        # No significant differences - set to NULL so no letters are shown
        pm25_sig_letters <- NULL
        cat("\nNo significant differences detected in pairwise comparisons. Skipping letters.\n")
      }
    } # end if emmeans
  } else {
    cat("Insufficient factor levels for PM2.5 ANCOVA (needed 2+ levels for both social_vuln_category and land_use_type).\n")
  }
  cat("\n")
}
}

# PM10 ANCOVA (controlling for covariates)
pm10_tukey <- NULL
pm10_sig_letters <- NULL
pm10_emmeans <- NULL
if(nrow(pm10_worst_month_data) > 0) {
  # Prepare data - ensure covariates are available and not NA
  pm10_model_data <- pm10_worst_month_data %>%
    filter(!is.na(aqi_pm10_corrected) & 
           !is.na(population_density) & 
           !is.na(land_use_type) & 
           !is.na(social_vuln_category))
  
  if(nrow(pm10_model_data) > 0) {
    # Fit ANCOVA model controlling for covariates (if factors have 2+ levels)
    sv_levels <- n_distinct(pm10_model_data$social_vuln_category)
    lu_levels <- n_distinct(pm10_model_data$land_use_type)
    
    if (sv_levels >= 2 && lu_levels >= 2) {
      pm10_ancova <- lm(aqi_pm10_corrected ~ social_vuln_category + 
                        population_density + land_use_type, 
                        data = pm10_model_data)
      pm10_ancova_summary <- summary(pm10_ancova)
      pm10_anova_table <- anova(pm10_ancova)
      
      cat("PM10 ANCOVA (", as.character(worst_month_pm10$month_name), ") - Controlling for population_density, land_use_type:\n")
      print(pm10_anova_table)
      cat("\nModel coefficients:\n")
      print(pm10_ancova_summary$coefficients)
      
      # Use emmeans for adjusted means and pairwise comparisons
      if(requireNamespace("emmeans", quietly = TRUE)) {
        pm10_emmeans <- emmeans::emmeans(pm10_ancova, ~ social_vuln_category)
      pm10_pairs <- pairs(pm10_emmeans, adjust = "tukey")
      
      cat("\nPM10 Adjusted means (controlling for covariates):\n")
      print(pm10_emmeans)
      
      cat("\nPM10 Pairwise comparisons (Tukey HSD, adjusted for covariates):\n")
      print(pm10_pairs)
      
      # Extract p-values for significance letters
      pairs_df <- as.data.frame(pm10_pairs)
      
      # Handle non-estimable comparisons (remove rows with nonEst or NA p-values)
      if("p.value" %in% names(pairs_df)) {
        # Convert character "nonEst" to NA
        if(is.character(pairs_df$p.value)) {
          pairs_df$p.value[pairs_df$p.value == "nonEst"] <- NA
        }
        # Convert to numeric, handling any remaining character values
        pairs_df$p.value <- as.numeric(pairs_df$p.value)
      }
      
      # Note about overall vs pairwise significance
      # Safely extract ANOVA p-value for social_vuln_category (Df == 2)
      anova_p_value <- tryCatch({
        pval <- pm10_anova_table[pm10_anova_table$Df == 2, "Pr(>F)"]
        if(length(pval) == 0) {
          NA_real_
        } else if(length(pval) > 1) {
          pval[1]  # Take first if multiple matches
        } else {
          as.numeric(pval)
        }
      }, error = function(e) {
        NA_real_
      })
      
      # Check if we have valid p-values for comparison
      has_valid_pairwise <- any(!is.na(pairs_df$p.value)) && any(pairs_df$p.value < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)
      has_valid_anova <- !is.na(anova_p_value) && is.finite(anova_p_value)
      
      if(has_valid_anova && anova_p_value < 0.05 && !has_valid_pairwise) {
        cat("\nNOTE: Overall ANCOVA shows significant effect of social vulnerability (p =", 
            round(anova_p_value, 4), 
            "), but no pairwise comparisons are significant after Tukey adjustment.\n")
        cat("This can occur when the effect is distributed across groups rather than concentrated in specific pairs,\n")
        cat("or when multiple testing correction makes pairwise tests more conservative.\n")
      }
      pm10_tukey <- list(social_vuln_category = pairs_df)
      rownames(pm10_tukey$social_vuln_category) <- as.character(pairs_df$contrast)
      # Ensure p adj column exists
      if("p.value" %in% names(pairs_df)) {
        pm10_tukey$social_vuln_category$`p adj` <- pairs_df$p.value
      } else {
        pm10_tukey$social_vuln_category$`p adj` <- rep(1, nrow(pairs_df))
      }
      
      # Get significance letters - only if there are significant differences
      if(any(pairs_df$p.value < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)) {
        pm10_sig_letters <- get_significance_letters(pm10_tukey, levels(pm10_model_data$social_vuln_category))
        # Check if all letters are the same - if so, no significant differences to display
        if(length(unique(pm10_sig_letters)) == 1) {
          pm10_sig_letters <- NULL
        }
      } else {
        # No significant differences - set to NULL so no letters are shown
        pm10_sig_letters <- NULL
        cat("\nNo significant differences detected in pairwise comparisons. Skipping letters.\n")
      }
    } # end if emmeans
  } else {
    cat("Insufficient factor levels for PM10 ANCOVA (needed 2+ levels for both social_vuln_category and land_use_type).\n")
  }
  cat("\n")
}
}

# Ozone ANCOVA (controlling for covariates)
ozone_tukey <- NULL
ozone_sig_letters <- NULL
ozone_emmeans <- NULL
if(nrow(ozone_worst_month_data) > 0) {
  # Prepare data - ensure covariates are available and not NA
  ozone_model_data <- ozone_worst_month_data %>%
    filter(!is.na(aqi_ozone_corrected) & 
           !is.na(population_density) & 
           !is.na(land_use_type) & 
           !is.na(social_vuln_category))
  
  if(nrow(ozone_model_data) > 0) {
    # Fit ANCOVA model controlling for covariates (if factors have 2+ levels)
    sv_levels <- n_distinct(ozone_model_data$social_vuln_category)
    lu_levels <- n_distinct(ozone_model_data$land_use_type)
    
    if (sv_levels >= 2 && lu_levels >= 2) {
      ozone_ancova <- lm(aqi_ozone_corrected ~ social_vuln_category + 
                         population_density + land_use_type, 
                         data = ozone_model_data)
      ozone_ancova_summary <- summary(ozone_ancova)
      ozone_anova_table <- anova(ozone_ancova)
      
      cat("Ozone ANCOVA (", as.character(worst_month_ozone$month_name), ") - Controlling for population_density, land_use_type:\n")
      print(ozone_anova_table)
      cat("\nModel coefficients:\n")
      print(ozone_ancova_summary$coefficients)
      
      # Use emmeans for adjusted means and pairwise comparisons
      if(requireNamespace("emmeans", quietly = TRUE)) {
        ozone_emmeans <- emmeans::emmeans(ozone_ancova, ~ social_vuln_category)
      ozone_pairs <- pairs(ozone_emmeans, adjust = "tukey")
      
      cat("\nOzone Adjusted means (controlling for covariates):\n")
      print(ozone_emmeans)
      
      cat("\nOzone Pairwise comparisons (Tukey HSD, adjusted for covariates):\n")
      print(ozone_pairs)
      
      # Extract p-values for significance letters
      pairs_df <- as.data.frame(ozone_pairs)
      
      # Handle non-estimable comparisons (remove rows with nonEst or NA p-values)
      if("p.value" %in% names(pairs_df)) {
        # Convert character "nonEst" to NA
        if(is.character(pairs_df$p.value)) {
          pairs_df$p.value[pairs_df$p.value == "nonEst"] <- NA
        }
        # Convert to numeric, handling any remaining character values
        pairs_df$p.value <- as.numeric(pairs_df$p.value)
      }
      
      # Note about overall vs pairwise significance
      # Safely extract ANOVA p-value for social_vuln_category (Df == 2)
      anova_p_value <- tryCatch({
        pval <- ozone_anova_table[ozone_anova_table$Df == 2, "Pr(>F)"]
        if(length(pval) == 0) {
          NA_real_
        } else if(length(pval) > 1) {
          pval[1]  # Take first if multiple matches
        } else {
          as.numeric(pval)
        }
      }, error = function(e) {
        NA_real_
      })
      
      # Check if we have valid p-values for comparison
      has_valid_pairwise <- any(!is.na(pairs_df$p.value)) && any(pairs_df$p.value < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)
      has_valid_anova <- !is.na(anova_p_value) && is.finite(anova_p_value)
      
      if(has_valid_anova && anova_p_value < 0.05 && !has_valid_pairwise) {
        cat("\nNOTE: Overall ANCOVA shows significant effect of social vulnerability (p =", 
            round(anova_p_value, 4), 
            "), but no pairwise comparisons are significant after Tukey adjustment.\n")
        cat("This can occur when the effect is distributed across groups rather than concentrated in specific pairs,\n")
        cat("or when multiple testing correction makes pairwise tests more conservative.\n")
      }
      ozone_tukey <- list(social_vuln_category = pairs_df)
      rownames(ozone_tukey$social_vuln_category) <- as.character(pairs_df$contrast)
      # Ensure p adj column exists
      if("p.value" %in% names(pairs_df)) {
        ozone_tukey$social_vuln_category$`p adj` <- pairs_df$p.value
      } else {
        ozone_tukey$social_vuln_category$`p adj` <- rep(1, nrow(pairs_df))
      }
      
      # Get significance letters - only if there are significant differences
      if(any(pairs_df$p.value < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)) {
        ozone_sig_letters <- get_significance_letters(ozone_tukey, levels(ozone_model_data$social_vuln_category))
        # Check if all letters are the same - if so, no significant differences to display
        if(length(unique(ozone_sig_letters)) == 1) {
          ozone_sig_letters <- NULL
        }
      } else {
        # No significant differences - set to NULL so no letters are shown
        ozone_sig_letters <- NULL
        cat("\nNo significant differences detected in pairwise comparisons. Skipping letters.\n")
      }
    } # end if emmeans
  } else {
    cat("Insufficient factor levels for Ozone ANCOVA (needed 2+ levels for both social_vuln_category and land_use_type).\n")
  }
  cat("\n")
}
}

# 4. Create publishable visualizations
cat("=== CREATING PUBLISHABLE VISUALIZATIONS ===\n")

# Combine data for worst months
worst_months_combined <- bind_rows(
  pm25_worst_month_data %>%
    dplyr::select(social_vuln_category, aqi = aqi_pm25_corrected, site, site_name) %>%
    mutate(pollutant = "PM2.5", worst_month = as.character(worst_month_pm25$month_name)),
  pm10_worst_month_data %>%
    dplyr::select(social_vuln_category, aqi = aqi_pm10_corrected, site, site_name) %>%
    mutate(pollutant = "PM10", worst_month = as.character(worst_month_pm10$month_name)),
  ozone_worst_month_data %>%
    dplyr::select(social_vuln_category, aqi = aqi_ozone_corrected, site, site_name) %>%
    mutate(pollutant = "Ozone", worst_month = as.character(worst_month_ozone$month_name))
) %>%
  filter(!is.na(aqi))

# Add significance letters to summaries - only show when there are significant differences
# For PM2.5
pm25_vuln_summary$sig_letter <- if(!is.null(pm25_sig_letters) && length(unique(pm25_sig_letters)) > 1) {
  letters <- pm25_sig_letters[as.character(pm25_vuln_summary$social_vuln_category)]
  ifelse(is.na(letters), "", letters)
} else { 
  rep("", nrow(pm25_vuln_summary))
}

# For PM10
pm10_vuln_summary$sig_letter <- if(!is.null(pm10_sig_letters) && length(unique(pm10_sig_letters)) > 1) {
  letters <- pm10_sig_letters[as.character(pm10_vuln_summary$social_vuln_category)]
  ifelse(is.na(letters), "", letters)
} else { 
  rep("", nrow(pm10_vuln_summary))
}

# For Ozone
ozone_vuln_summary$sig_letter <- if(!is.null(ozone_sig_letters) && length(unique(ozone_sig_letters)) > 1) {
  letters <- ozone_sig_letters[as.character(ozone_vuln_summary$social_vuln_category)]
  ifelse(is.na(letters), "", letters)
} else { 
  rep("", nrow(ozone_vuln_summary))
}

# Combine summaries
vuln_summary_temp <- bind_rows(
  pm25_vuln_summary %>%
    mutate(pollutant = "PM2.5", worst_month = as.character(worst_month_pm25$month_name)),
  pm10_vuln_summary %>%
    mutate(pollutant = "PM10", worst_month = as.character(worst_month_pm10$month_name)),
  ozone_vuln_summary %>%
    mutate(pollutant = "Ozone", worst_month = as.character(worst_month_ozone$month_name))
)

# Calculate pct_diff_from_low from scratch, ensuring it exists
vuln_summary_combined <- vuln_summary_temp %>%
  group_by(pollutant) %>%
  mutate(
    low_mean_aqi = mean_aqi[social_vuln_category == "Low"][1]
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    pct_diff_from_low = ifelse(
      !is.na(low_mean_aqi) & low_mean_aqi > 0 & !is.na(mean_aqi),
      round(100 * (mean_aqi - low_mean_aqi) / low_mean_aqi, 1),
      NA_real_
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(-low_mean_aqi)

# PUBLISHABLE GRAPHIC 1: Mean AQI with disparities highlighted
# Create subtitle with worst month info
worst_month_subtitle <- paste0(
  "PM2.5: ", worst_month_pm25$month_name, 
  " | PM10: ", worst_month_pm10$month_name,
  " | Ozone: ", worst_month_ozone$month_name
)

# Prepare data for plotting with proper error bar limits and label positioning
publishable_disparities_data <- vuln_summary_combined %>%
  mutate(
    pollutant = factor(pollutant, levels = c("PM2.5", "PM10", "Ozone")),
    social_vuln_category = factor(social_vuln_category, levels = c("Low", "Moderate", "High")),
```
